Elevate Dashboard Metrics Update

Career Services & Experiential Learning · Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform Product

Dashboard Overview

Workforce Development & Career Readiness · Hybrid Skills Product
Badge system, framework alignment, program metrics, and product design
Updated May 2026

Strategic positioning · May 2026

Elevate. Illinois Tech’s Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform.

The institutional spine for career readiness, workforce development, academic integration, lifelong learning, and corporate partnership — built on Illinois Tech’s 1890 promise of learning by doing.

Learner Population I
On-campus Illinois Tech students
A four-year career-readiness ladder — credentialed, badge-verified, and employer-visible from day one.
Learner Population II
37.6M+ adults ·
“Some College, No Credential”
1.1M in Illinois — Americans who enrolled in college but never earned a credential (National Student Clearinghouse).
Learner Population III
Global learners · 100+ countries
A globally diverse research university — expert at delivering cutting-edge learning, career readiness, and verifiable credentialing worldwide.
The Elevate Guarantee

Complete every Elevate requirement and don’t land an offer within six months of graduation? Eligibility for up to 9 graduate credit hours tuition-free, or one term of cost-free upskilling or digital apprenticeship. Available within one year of graduation; subject to Illinois Tech terms.

Career Readiness Framework Integration

Elevate badges align with nationally recognized academic and professional competency frameworks

P21 21st Century Skills

Framework Coverage 95%

NACE Career Readiness

Competency Alignment 92%

WEF Future of Jobs 2030

Skills Coverage 88%

CASEL SEL Framework

Competency Integration 78%

Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report

Industry Job-Readiness Signals 90%

Integrated institutional frameworks — the research-grade anchors built into Elevate across badge design, activity catalog, assessment forms, and reporting. Each shows its badge-alignment percentage.

Harvard GSE — Pathways to Prosperity

Pre-college through Employment Pathway85%

National framework advocating structured pathways from secondary education through postsecondary into careers. Aligns with Elevate’s pre-college through employment-guarantee pipeline.

HBS Managing the Future of Work

Skills-Based Hiring Alignment80%

Evidence base for skills-based hiring, experiential credentialing, and employer engagement informing Elevate’s badge system and employment-guarantee model.

MIT Experiential Learning Office (ELO)

Multi-Pathway Badge Progression90%

ELO portfolio model and assessment framework inform Elevate’s multi-pathway badge progression and competency-verification methodology.

MIT T+L Lab — Digital Credentialing

Open Badges / Verifiable Credentials95%

Pioneer in digital credentialing and open badges. Foundation for Elevate’s digital-badge architecture, portable credentials, and skills verification.

Oxford Careers — Skills-Based Career Dev

Transferable Skills Mapping82%

Competency framework mapping transferable skills across academic disciplines. Mirrors Elevate’s co-curricular integration strategy.

NACE Career Readiness Competencies

8 Competency Coverage92%

Eight competencies: Career & Self-Dev, Communication, Critical Thinking, Equity & Inclusion, Leadership, Professionalism, Teamwork, Technology. Core reference for Elevate’s five-skill badge system.

AAC&U High-Impact Practices (HIPs)

HIPs Embedded in Activities82%

First-year seminars, learning communities, writing-intensive courses, collaborative assignments, undergraduate research, diversity/global learning, internships, capstones. Multiple HIPs embedded across Elevate.

Northeastern Co-op & Experiential Learning

Work-Integrated Learning Model88%

Gold standard for cooperative education with 100+ years of practice. Evidence base for Elevate’s employment-guarantee approach.

Purdue-Gallup Index

Mentorship & Engagement Outcomes78%

Longitudinal study connecting experiential learning to long-term career outcomes. Demonstrates that mentorship, applied projects, and deep engagement predict career satisfaction and economic mobility.

Cincinnati Cooperative Education

Institutional-Scale Co-op Benchmark75%

Birthplace of cooperative education (1906). Institutional-scale model of mandatory co-op experiences — historical and operational benchmark for Elevate’s program-wide approach.

Georgia Tech — Experiential Education

STEM Career Preparation Alignment85%

STEM-focused experiential education integrating research, internships, and co-op. Mirrors Elevate’s AWS Academy integration and Armour engineering partnerships.

Kolb’s Experiential Learning Theory (ELT)

Reflection Cycle Coverage100%

Concrete experience → reflective observation → abstract conceptualization → active experimentation. All Elevate reflections and badge progressions map to Kolb’s four-stage cycle.

NACE 2026 Job Outlook + Coursera Job Skills 2026 — Why This Matters for Elevate

Updated May 2026

The 2026 NACE Job Outlook (Spring Update, n=185) projects a 5.6% hiring increase for the Class of 2026. Demand for AI skills more than doubled in six months — 35% of entry-level jobs now require AI skills — and 57.8% of employers assign interns AI projects. Most importantly, large gaps persist between what employers say is important and the proficiency they observe in recent graduates. Elevate’s required activities, badge tracks, and Workshop Series are designed to close exactly these gaps.

Importance vs Proficiency (% of NACE employers rating “very/extremely”)

NACE CompetencyImportanceProficiencyGapElevate badge / activity that closes it
Communication98.7%55.4%−43.3Professional Communications L1–L5; Workshops 2 (CAR), 5 (Info Interviews), 9 (Personal Brand), 11 (Interview)
Critical Thinking93.5%50.0%−43.5Career Critical Thinker L1–L5; Industry Research Deep Dive; Case Competitions
Professionalism94.1%54.7%−53.4Mock Interview, Career Fair Prep Squad, Stuart CMC Coaching
Career & Self-Development48.7%37.2%−11.5Co-Curricular Plan; Flight Plan; Self-Awareness L1–L5
Teamwork96.1%75.0%−21.1IPRO Innovation Day; Hackathons; Startup Studio
Technology75.2%65.5%−9.7AWS Cloud Skills; Digital Literacy L1–L5; Tech+ Specialist
Leadership41.8%31.1%−10.7ELM Leadership; Student Org Leadership; PM Excellence Club
AI Skills18.4%25.3%+6.9Workshop 4 (Applying with AI); AI Practitioner badge; AWS AI Foundations

Top resume attributes employers actively seek (NACE 2026)

Ability to work in a team (85%), Problem-solving (82%), Communication verbal (78%), Technical skills (73%), Communication written (72%), Strong work ethic (71%), Analytical/quantitative (70%), Initiative (69%), Leadership potential (68%), Detail-oriented (65%). 59% of employers want specific skills with examples — the CAR Method workshop is built around exactly that. The Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report reinforces this trajectory: AI literacy, data analytics, and human-centered communication are the fastest-rising entry-level expectations across global hiring markets.

Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report — Why This Matters for Elevate

Updated May 2026

Coursera publishes an annual Job Skills Report drawing on enrollment and completion behavior across its global learner base and demand signals from its employer partners. The 2024 edition famously highlighted a ~866% year-over-year growth in Generative AI course enrollments — the single largest skill-demand shift Coursera had measured to that point. The 2025 and 2026 editions continued to rank Generative AI, Data Analytics, Cybersecurity, Cloud Computing, and Human-Centered Communication at the top of the entry-level demand curve. Elevate’s badge framework is designed to convert this directional signal into verifiable, employer-aligned credentials for Illinois Tech students.

Top Entry-Level Skill Priorities (Coursera Job Skills Report, 2024–2026)

Skill DomainCoursera SignalElevate badge / activity that closes it
Generative AI & Prompt EngineeringFastest-rising skill of the cycle — cited at ~866% YoY enrollment growth in the 2024 report; remains #1 priority in 2025 & 2026 editions.Workshop 4 (Applying with AI); AI Practitioner L1–L5; AWS AI Foundations
Data Analytics & Business IntelligenceTop-2 fastest-growing technical skill across all three recent Coursera Job Skills Reports.Digital Literacy L3–L4; Marketing Analytics Project; Data Storytelling elective; Tech+ Specialist
Cybersecurity FundamentalsTop-5 fastest-growing across the 2024–2026 reports; tied to rapid expansion of SOC and Zero Trust enrollments.AWS Security Specialty; ITM / Cybersecurity pathway; Digital Literacy L3+
Cloud Computing & ArchitecturePersistent top-5 demand domain across the report series, driven by AWS / Azure / GCP partner enrollment.AWS Cloud Foundations → Developing → Architecting; AWS exam discount; Future CTO pathway
Human-Centered CommunicationDurable human skill; consistently flagged as essential alongside AI literacy. Cross-references the NACE 98.7% Communication importance signal.Professional Communications L1–L5; Workshops 2 (CAR), 9 (Personal Brand), 11 (Interview)
Project Management & AgileSustained mid-tier demand across editions; CAPM and Scrum cited as the most-pursued certifications.PM Excellence Club; CAPM Certification (auto-earn Future COO); Stuart Tech+ project
Machine Learning & PythonPersistent foundational-technical demand; Python remains the most-enrolled programming language across all Coursera reports.AWS Machine Learning Foundations; CS / AI majors; Industry Certification track
Critical Thinking & Problem SolvingDurable human skill; cross-references NACE 93.5% Critical Thinking importance signal.Career Critical Thinker L1–L5; Industry Research Deep Dive; Business Case Competitions
Leadership & Change ManagementDurable leadership domain; consistently top-10 across all editions.ELM Leadership; Leadership Academy (6 workshops); Student Org Leadership
Digital Marketing & ContentSteady top-10 specialization across editions; tied to LinkedIn & marketing certificate demand.AMA Membership; Marketing Analytics Project; LinkedIn Profile Optimization; Future CMO pathway

Illinois Tech × Coursera — Scale & Integration

Career Academy
Illinois Tech Coursera Career Academy — institutional subscription serving currently-enrolled students & alumni
~150M

7,000+
Coursera business / campus customers cited in the 2024 report
Internal
Coursera priority-skill crosswalk against the Elevate badge catalog — under active maintenance

What This Means for Elevate Programming

Generative AI is the “non-negotiable” skill for the Class of 2026. The 866% YoY GenAI enrollment surge from Coursera’s 2024 report mirrors the NACE 2026 finding that 35% of entry-level jobs now require AI skills and 57.8% of employers assign interns AI projects. Elevate’s Workshop 4 (Applying with AI), the AI Practitioner badge track, and the AWS AI Foundations pathway are designed to convert this demand into a verifiable credential before graduation.

Durable human skills still anchor the top of the demand curve. Communication, Critical Thinking, and Leadership continue to feature prominently in the Coursera reports even as technical skills surge. This validates Elevate’s five-skill foundational badge model (Professional Communications, Career Critical Thinker, Collaboration Champion, Leadership, Digital Literacy) and confirms that the Workshop Series and Elevate Success Coach 1:1s are the right delivery mechanism.

Coursera-Illinois Tech is the lifelong-learning & non-credit pillar. The Coursera Job Skills Report series is the third anchor framework for Elevate alongside NACE Career Readiness and the WEF Future of Jobs 2030. The Global Career Accelerator / Podium activity (Tier 3, 3 credits, ~45 hr) is the direct on-ramp from Coursera enrollment to Elevate badge earning.

Sources & verification. Specific YoY growth figures are drawn from Coursera’s published Job Skills Reports (2024, 2025, 2026 editions); the 866% GenAI figure is from the 2024 edition. Domain priority rankings are directional (top-10 placement) rather than precise growth percentages. Illinois Tech’s Coursera footprint is provisioned through the Coursera Career Academy — internal enrollment counts should be confirmed with the Office of Digital Learning. The badge-to-Coursera crosswalk is maintained internally by Career Services & Experiential Learning.

Learning Hours Tier Framework

Badges are organized into five tiers based on learning hours and skill demonstration requirements. The tier model applies to the 25 foundational badges; milestone and specialized badges layer on top for cross-cutting and partnership-specific achievements.

L1
Foundation
1-5 hours
L2
Skill Builder
5-15 hours
L3
Proficient
15-40 hours
L4
Advanced
40-60 hours
L5
Elevator
60-100+ hrs

Milestone Badges — 20 cross-cutting achievements

Single-level achievement badges that recognize specific accomplishments and category-defining experiences. Four color-coded categories.

Green & Gold

Program Progression — 4

Initiative Taker · Halfway Hero · Year 1 Completer · Year 2 Completer

Orange & Gold

Career Preparedness — 6

Resume Builder · Career Explorer · Mentorship Match · Interview Ready · Network Navigator · Career Strategist

Purple & Gold

Character Development — 5

Global Citizen · Community Champion · Resilience Builder · Ethical Leader · Inclusive Advocate

Scarlet & Gold

Special Achievements — 5

Cloud Foundations · Innovation Pioneer · Published Professional · Certification Achiever · Elevator Award

Specialized Badges — 28 partnership tracks

Partnership-specific badges across school-level integrations, employer pathways, and thematic tracks.

Black & Gold

Stuart C-Suite Pathway — 5

Future CEO · COO (auto: CAPM) · CFO (auto: SIE 70%+) · CTO · CMO

Cloud & AI

AWS Academy — 17 pathways

Foundations (Cloud, AI, ML, Security) plus advanced tracks (Architecting, Operations, Data Engineering, NLP, more)

Bridge

Transfer Cohort — 8 (proposed 9)

Accelerated progression for transfer students; credit for prior experiential learning

Proposed

Pre-Health Partnership

Clinical exposure, research, and health-career exploration mapped to Elevate competencies

Emerging

Esports & Digital Arts

Game design, esports management, digital content creation aligned with Illinois Tech’s gaming programming

Framework Alignment

How Elevate badges map to nationally and internationally recognized career-readiness, experiential-learning, digital-credentialing, and transfer-specific frameworks · 20+ grounding frameworks across five evidence pillars.

Elevate’s 73-badge architecture is grounded in twenty-plus externally validated frameworks drawn from career-readiness research, experiential-learning theory, digital-credentialing standards, leading research universities, and transfer-specific scholarship. Every badge, every activity, and every reflection prompt traces back to at least one source below.

The list is organized into five evidence pillars: (1) Career-readiness competency frameworks, (2) Experiential-learning theory and high-impact-practices research, (3) Digital-credentialing standards, (4) Institutional models from leading research universities, and (5) Transfer-specific scholarship.

Headline Coverage — Core Career-Readiness Frameworks

Five anchor frameworks define the Elevate measurement baseline; badge-alignment percentages indicate the share of each framework’s constructs explicitly mapped to an Elevate badge or activity.

P21 21st Century Skills

Framework Coverage95%

Partnership for 21st Century Learning (2019). The 4 Cs (Critical Thinking, Communication, Collaboration, Creativity) + life and career skills + information / media / ICT literacy.

NACE Career Readiness Competencies

Competency Alignment92%

National Association of Colleges and Employers (2024). 8 competencies: Career & Self-Development, Communication, Critical Thinking, Equity & Inclusion, Leadership, Professionalism, Teamwork, Technology.

WEF Future of Jobs 2030

Top-15 Skills Coverage88%

World Economic Forum (2025). Analytical Thinking, AI & Big Data, Tech Literacy, Active Learning, Resilience, Empathy, Curiosity, Leadership and Social Influence.

AAC&U High-Impact Practices

HIPs Embedded82%

Kuh, G. D. (2008). First-year experiences, learning communities, writing-intensive courses, collaborative assignments, undergraduate research, diversity / global learning, internships, capstones.

O*NET / U.S. Department of Labor

Occupational Skills Coverage85%

U.S. Department of Labor (2024). O*NET Online Skills Search; RIASEC Interest Profiler; Work Importance Locator — public-domain career-development instruments.

CASEL Social-Emotional Learning

5-Competency Integration78%

Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning (2023). 5 SEL competencies: Self-Awareness, Self-Management, Social Awareness, Relationship Skills, Responsible Decision-Making.

U.S. DOE Employability Skills

Employability Coverage75%

U.S. Department of Education (2018). Applied academic skills, critical-thinking skills, interpersonal skills, personal qualities, technology use, systems thinking, communication.

Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report

Industry Signal Alignment90%

Coursera (2024–2026 editions). GenAI literacy, Data Analytics, Cybersecurity, Cloud, Human-Centered Communication. The lifelong-learning & non-credit pillar anchor.

Kolb’s Experiential Learning Theory

Reflection Cycle Coverage100%

Kolb, D. A. (2015). Concrete Experience → Reflective Observation → Abstract Conceptualization → Active Experimentation. Every Elevate reflection prompt anchors to one Kolb stage.

FrameworkConstructs Mapped to ElevateCoverage

CASEL SEL Framework (2023)Self-Awareness, Self-Management, Social Awareness, Relationship Skills, Responsible Decision-Making. Anchors Elevate Success Coach check-ins and reflection design.78%

Pillar 2 — Experiential-Learning Theory & High-Impact Practices

Source / TheoryHow It Shapes Elevate
Kolb, D. A. (2015) — Experiential Learning TheoryConcrete Experience → Reflective Observation → Abstract Conceptualization → Active Experimentation. The 300–500 word reflection requirement on every activity closes the Kolb cycle.
Kuh, G. D. (2008) — High-Impact Educational PracticesAAC&U publication enumerating the 11 HIPs. Elevate embeds first-year experience, learning communities, writing-intensive activities, undergraduate research, diversity / global learning, internships, capstones, and service learning.
AAC&U (2018) — Fulfilling the American DreamLiberal education and the future of work. Frames Elevate as the institutional response to the AAC&U call for purposeful integration of liberal-education outcomes with employer-recognized credentials.
Hora, M. T., Wolfgram, M., & Thompson, S. (2017)Center for Research on College-Workforce Transitions. Reviewed evidence on internship impact on student outcomes — informs the Elevate internship-quality rubric and outcomes-tracking design.
Anderson & Krathwohl (2001) — Bloom’s Revised TaxonomyProvides the cognitive-progression model behind the L1 (Foundation) → L5 (Elevator) tier escalation in the foundational badge architecture.
Dreyfus, S. E. (1986) — Five-Stage Model of Skill AcquisitionNovice → Advanced Beginner → Competent → Proficient → Expert. The conceptual backbone for the five-tier badge progression mapped onto NACE / WEF / CASEL constructs.
Tinto, V. (2012) — Completing CollegeRethinking institutional action for student persistence. Informs the Elevate Success Coach + cohort-community design as the institutional retention layer.

Pillar 3 — Digital Credentialing & Open Badges Standards

Standard / SourceApplication to Elevate
Open Badges 3.0 (1EdTech / W3C Verifiable Credentials)Machine-readable, employer-verifiable badge format. Foundation for the Elevate Verifiable Credential issuance pipeline (planned via Talon backend, Fall 2027 target).
Mozilla Foundation & Peer 2 Peer University (2012)Open Badges for Lifelong Learning. The originating publication for the open-badge ecosystem in which Elevate’s 73-badge framework participates.
Carey, K. L., & Stefaniak, J. E. (2018)Empirical study on the utility of digital badging in higher-education settings. Cited as evidence base for Elevate badge-design choices.
Ifenthaler, Bellin-Mularski & Mah (2016)Foundation of Digital Badges and Micro-Credentials (Springer). Foundational reference for the conceptual architecture of stackable micro-credentials — the model behind the C-Suite Pathway stacked badges.
MIT Digital Credentials ConsortiumMulti-institutional standards body. Elevate is the campus-scale implementation site for the MIT-led Verifiable Credentials standards.

Pillar 4 — Institutional Models from Leading Research Universities

Institution & ModelInfluence on ElevateAlignment
Harvard GSE — Pathways to ProsperityNational framework advocating structured pathways from secondary education through postsecondary into careers. Anchors Elevate’s Pre-College through employment-guarantee pipeline.85%
HBS Managing the Future of WorkResearch initiative on skills-based hiring, experiential credentialing, and employer engagement. Evidence base for Elevate’s badge system + employment-guarantee model.80%
MIT Experiential Learning Office (ELO)Multi-pathway portfolio model coordinating UROP, MISTI, Sloan Action Learning, MITdesignX. Direct template for Elevate’s “menu of activities anchored to a single framework” design.90%
MIT T+L Lab — Digital CredentialingPioneer in digital credentialing and Open Badges research. Foundation for Elevate’s digital-badge architecture, portable credentials, and Open Badges 3.0 roadmap.95%
Oxford University Careers ServiceSkills-Based Career Development framework mapping transferable skills across disciplines. Mirrors Elevate’s co-curricular integration strategy.82%
Northeastern University — Co-op Model100+ years of cooperative education with 4,000+ employer partners. Evidence base for Elevate’s employment-guarantee model and structured work-integrated learning.88%
University of Cincinnati — Cooperative EducationOriginator of cooperative education (1906). Institutional-scale benchmark for Elevate’s program-wide approach.75%
Purdue-Gallup IndexGallup & Purdue University (2014). Longitudinal study connecting experiential learning, mentorship, and applied projects to long-term career outcomes and well-being.78%
Georgia Institute of Technology — Experiential EducationSTEM-focused integration of undergraduate research, internships, and co-op. Mirrors Elevate’s AWS Academy + Armour engineering partnerships.85%
University of Waterloo Co-opWorld’s largest post-secondary co-op program. Benchmark for global scale of work-integrated learning.Benchmark
Carnegie Mellon · Stanford Career Education · ETH Zurich · NUS · Tokyo · Melbourne · Imperial College LondonCross-institutional benchmarks for career-services architecture, project-based learning, employer engagement, and longitudinal outcomes tracking. See Metrics → Competitive Landscape for the full 5-category analysis.Comparative

Pillar 5 — Transfer-Specific Scholarship & Instruments

SourceHow It Anchors the Transfer Cohort Bridge Pathway
Laanan, Starobin & Eggleston (2010) — Transfer Student CapitalCore asset-based framework: transfers arrive with accumulated knowledge that predicts persistence and self-efficacy when explicitly surfaced.
Schlossberg (1981) — Transition TheoryMoving-In / Moving-Through / Moving-Out + the 4 S’s (Situation, Self, Support, Strategies). Structures the bridge-year coaching cadence.
Jain, Bernal Melendez & Herrera (2020) — Transfer Receptive CultureReframes transfer success as institutional responsibility, not student responsibility. The cultural ethos behind the “You’re Not Behind. You’re Ready.” promise.
Wyner, Deane, Jenkins & Fink (2016) — Transfer PlaybookCCRC / Aspen. Tailored advising + prior-learning credit review as essential evidence-based practices for high-transfer-success institutions.
Monaghan & Attewell (2015) — Credit-Loss ResearchEmpirical case for ~50% credit loss during institutional handoff. The Activity 3 Prior Learning & Experience Audit directly addresses this loss.
Ishitani (2008) — Transfer-Shock ResearchLongitudinal documentation of first-term GPA dip post-transfer. Justifies front-loading Activities 1–3 in the bridge year’s first six weeks.
Velasco, Fink & Jenkins (2024) — Tracking TransferCCRC / Aspen / NSC. Sets the 16% / 9–13% community-college-to-bachelor’s completion benchmark against which the Transfer Cohort 85%+ goal is measured.
Strada Education Foundation (2023) — Transfer Mobility ReportCredit-portability data informing the Prior Learning & Experience Audit worksheet design.
Lukszo & Hayes (2020) — Sources of Transfer Student CapitalExtension of Laanan’s TSC framework with empirical evidence on which transfer-specific resources predict bachelor’s completion.
Moser (2012) — L-TSQ Validated InstrumentLaanan-Transfer Students’ Questionnaire. Pre / post administration provides the validated longitudinal measurement instrument for the bridge year.

Workforce-Development & Technology Frameworks

SourceApplication
Amazon Web Services (2024) — AWS Academy17 certification pathways integrated as Elevate Tier 3 / Tier 4 specialized badges; 50% exam-discount benefit for Illinois Tech students.
Burning Glass Technologies (2019) / LightcastThe Hybrid Job Economy. Real-time labor-market data informing quarterly cross-walk of the Elevate badge catalog against current employer demand signals.
Chicago Quantum Exchange (2024) — Q-Ready WorkforceQuantum-computing workforce-development initiative. Foundation for the planned Elevate Quantum specialized badge track.

Reference List (APA 7th ed.)

Formatted according to American Psychological Association (2020). Publication manual of the American Psychological Association (7th ed.). A complete, page-specific bibliography is maintained on the dashboard References page and at elevateillinoistech.github.io/ElevateDashboard0226/.

Anderson, L. W., & Krathwohl, D. R. (Eds.). (2001). A taxonomy for learning, teaching, and assessing: A revision of Bloom’s taxonomy of educational objectives (Complete ed.). Longman.

Amazon Web Services. (2024). AWS Academy. https://aws.amazon.com/training/awsacademy/

Association of American Colleges and Universities. (2018). Fulfilling the American dream: Liberal education and the future of work. AAC&U.

Burning Glass Technologies. (2019). The hybrid job economy: How new skills are rewriting the DNA of the job market.

Carey, K. L., & Stefaniak, J. E. (2018). An exploration of the utility of digital badging in higher education settings. Educational Technology Research and Development, 66(5), 1211–1229.

Chicago Quantum Exchange. (2024). Quantum workforce development initiative. University of Chicago.

Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning. (2023). CASEL’s SEL framework.

Coursera. (2024, 2025, 2026). Job Skills Reports.

Dreyfus, S. E. (1986). Mind over machine: The power of human intuition and expertise in the era of the computer. Free Press.

Gallup & Purdue University. (2014). Great jobs, great lives: The 2014 Gallup-Purdue index report.

Hora, M. T., Wolfgram, M., & Thompson, S. (2017). What do we know about the impact of internships on student outcomes? CCWT Research Brief, (2), 1–20.

Ifenthaler, D., Bellin-Mularski, N., & Mah, D. K. (Eds.). (2016). Foundation of digital badges and micro-credentials. Springer.

Ishitani, T. T. (2008). How do transfers survive after “transfer shock”? Research in Higher Education, 49(5), 403–419.

Jain, D., Bernal Melendez, S. N., & Herrera, A. R. (2020). Power to the transfer: Critical race theory and a transfer receptive culture. Michigan State University Press.

Kolb, D. A. (2015). Experiential learning: Experience as the source of learning and development (2nd ed.). Pearson Education.

Kuh, G. D. (2008). High-impact educational practices: What they are, who has access to them, and why they matter. AAC&U.

Laanan, F. S., Starobin, S. S., & Eggleston, L. E. (2010). Adjustment of community college students at a four-year university. Journal of College Student Retention, 12(2), 175–209.

Lukszo, C. M., & Hayes, S. (2020). Facilitating transfer student success. Community College Review, 48(1), 31–54.

Monaghan, D. B., & Attewell, P. (2015). The community college route to the bachelor’s degree. Educational Evaluation and Policy Analysis, 37(1), 70–91.

Moser, K. M. (2012). Redefining transfer student success: Transfer capital and the Laanan-Transfer Students’ Questionnaire (L-TSQ) revisited [Doctoral dissertation, Iowa State University].

Mozilla Foundation & Peer 2 Peer University. (2012). Open badges for lifelong learning.

National Association of Colleges and Employers. (2024). Career readiness: Competencies for a career-ready workforce. NACE.

National Association of Colleges and Employers. (2026). Job Outlook 2026 (Spring update). NACE.

Partnership for 21st Century Learning. (2019). Framework for 21st century learning. Battelle for Kids.

Schlossberg, N. K. (1981). A model for analyzing human adaptation to transition. The Counseling Psychologist, 9(2), 2–18.

Strada Education Foundation. (2023). Transfer mobility report.

Tinto, V. (2012). Completing college: Rethinking institutional action. University of Chicago Press.

U.S. Department of Education. (2018). Employability skills framework.

U.S. Department of Labor. (2024). O*NET Online: Skills search.

Velasco, T., Fink, J., & Jenkins, D. (2024). Tracking transfer. CCRC, Aspen Institute, NSC Research Center.

World Economic Forum. (2025). The future of jobs report 2025.

Wyner, J., Deane, K. C., Jenkins, D., & Fink, J. (2016). The transfer playbook: Essential practices for two- and four-year colleges. Aspen Institute / CCRC.

Milestone & Achievement Badges

20 achievement badges awarded for program progression, career preparedness, character development, and special accomplishments. Milestone badges recognize holistic growth beyond individual skill competencies.

Total Milestone Badges
20
Across 4 categories
Program Progression
4
Pre-College through Graduation
Career Preparedness
6
Job-readiness milestones
Character & Special
10
Development & achievement

Program Progression Badges (4)

Automatically awarded when students complete all required activities for each program year. These badges mark major Elevate milestones and are prerequisites for the employment guarantee.

BadgeCriteriaRequirementsGuarantee Impact
Pre-College PioneerComplete pre-college program requirementsParticipation in Illinois Tech pre-collegiate program (SCA, CPIE, Project Muse, or equivalent); completion of pre-college reflection and artifact upload.Early entry into Elevate pipeline; priority coaching assignment.
First-Year Foundation BuilderComplete all Year 1 required activitiesT&C signed; Elevate Success Coach Check-In; Co-Curricular Plan submitted; Resume Development; Experience completion; Find Your Flock; Mentorship Program; Chicago Adventures.Unlocks Year 2 activities and elective options.
Sophomore Success AchieverComplete all Year 2 required activitiesSophomore Co-Curricular Plan; Handshake optimization; LinkedIn profile; Resume review; Elevate Success Coach Check-In; Elevate Experience; Professional Development Workshop; Leadership Activity.Unlocks Year 3–4 advanced tracks.
Elevate GraduateFull program completionAll required activities across all program years completed; minimum badge portfolio achieved; final reflection and career-readiness assessment.Full employment-guarantee eligibility activated.

Career Preparedness Badges (6)

Awarded when students demonstrate specific job-readiness competencies through accumulated activities and artifacts. These badges signal employer-ready skills.

BadgeCriteriaHow to EarnQualifying Activities
Experience Collector5+ diverse experiential activities across categoriesComplete activities spanning at least 3 different experience types (internship, research, service, competition, simulation, etc.)Any combination from Elevate Activities Catalog across multiple categories
Job Search Portfolio StrategistComplete professional portfolioApproved resume, cover letter, LinkedIn profile, Handshake profile, and mock interview completionResume Dev, LinkedIn Profile, Handshake Optimization, Mock Interview, Cover Letter Workshop
Industry NavigatorDemonstrate industry knowledgeComplete 2+ informational interviews and 1 industry research project or job shadowInformational Interviews, Job Shadowing, Industry Research Project, Employer Info Sessions
Certification AchieverEarn external industry certificationComplete at least 1 industry-recognized certification (AWS, Salesforce, Google, Microsoft, etc.)AWS Cloud Practitioner, Salesforce Foundations, Google Analytics, CAPM, SIE Exam
Internship / Co-Op ChampionSecure and complete work experienceComplete a paid or credit-bearing internship or co-op experience with employer evaluationCo-Op program, Paid Internship, Virtual Internship (Forage), Research Assistantship
Career Fair StrategistStrategic career-fair engagementAttend 2+ career fairs with pre-event target employer list and post-event follow-up documentationFall Career Fair, Spring Career Fair, Stuart Career Fair, Industry-Specific Fairs

Character Development Badges (5)

Recognize personal growth, professional identity development, and soft-skill mastery demonstrated through patterns of engagement rather than single activities.

BadgeCriteriaHow to EarnEvidence Required
Initiative TakerSelf-directed pursuit beyond requirementsComplete 3+ elective activities not required by program year. Must be self-initiated, not coach-assigned.Activity completion records, elective submissions; coach attestation of self-direction.
Professional Relationship BuilderNetwork developmentComplete mentorship program + 2 networking events + 1 informational interviewMentorship completion, event attendance records, informational-interview reflection.
Self-Awareness ChampionReflective practice and growth mindsetComplete 3+ reflective activities with documented learning insights and career-goal evolutionReflection artifacts, Co-Curricular Plan updates, coaching session notes.
Resilience & Persistence LeaderSustained engagement through challengesMaintain active Elevate participation across 3+ consecutive semesters with documented challenge navigationMulti-semester activity records, coach attestation, reflection on overcoming obstacles.
Cultural Bridge BuilderCross-cultural competence demonstrated through activityComplete 2+ activities that engage diverse perspectives or international contexts; reflective writing required.Study away reflection, Globe Trotter reflection, Service Learning Abroad documentation, Stuart Global Business Navigator.

Special Achievement Badges (5)

Recognize exceptional accomplishments in specific domains. These badges highlight unique contributions and specialized experiences that distinguish students in the job market.

BadgeCriteriaHow to EarnQualifying Activities
Global CitizenInternational / cross-cultural engagementComplete 1+ international or cross-cultural experience with reflectionStudy Away (any program), Globe Trotter, Career Accelerator, International Student Mentoring, Cultural Immersion Events
Innovation PioneerOriginal problem-solving / venture creationLead or co-lead a project that produces a novel artifact, prototype, or ventureIPRO Innovation Day winner, Kaplan Startup Studio venture, Hackathon project, Entrepreneurial Venture Development
Community Impact LeaderSustained service and community engagement15+ hours of community / service engagement across multiple events with reflectionBronzeville Service Day, Hope Chicago, Chicago Difference, Service Learning Abroad
Research ScholarOriginal research contributionAuthor or co-author research project or paper; faculty / supervisor sign-off requiredUndergraduate Research, Research Abroad, IPRO Research Track, Argonne / Fermilab placement
Future C-Suite (Stuart only)Stuart C-Suite Pathway completion (any of CEO / COO / CFO / CTO / CMO)Complete the curated activity sequence for one of the Stuart C-Suite pathways; auto-earn triggers (CAPM → COO; SIE 70%+ → CFO) apply.Stuart Professional Certificate + Entrepreneurial Venture Development + Leadership L3+; or Industry Certification auto-earn trigger

Milestone Badge Earning Summary

Badges by Category

Earning Difficulty & Impact

CategoryAvg. TimeDifficultyEmployer Signal
Program Progression1–4 semestersCumulativeProgram commitment & completion
Career Preparedness2–8 weeks eachModerateJob-ready skills verified
Character Development1–3 semestersBehavioralProfessional maturity & growth
Special AchievementVariesSelectiveDistinctive accomplishments

How Milestone Badges Connect to the Employment Guarantee

Required for guarantee: All 4 Program Progression badges (Pre-College Pioneer through Elevate Graduate) must be earned. Students must also earn a minimum of 3 Career Preparedness badges and 2 Character Development badges.

Enhanced guarantee: Students earning 15+ total milestone badges receive priority placement support, extended guarantee timeline, and access to premium employer partnerships.

Badge portfolio: All earned milestone badges are displayed on the student’s Elevate profile, shared via Credly digital credentials, and included in Career Services employer referral packages.

Specialized Badges

28 partnership-anchored badges that sit on top of the foundational and milestone systems — Stuart C-Suite, AWS Academy, Transfer Cohort, Pre-Health (proposed), and Esports & Digital Arts (emerging).

Total Specialized Badges
28
Across 5 partnership tracks
Stuart C-Suite (Black & Gold)
5
Future CEO · COO · CFO · CTO · CMO
AWS Academy
17
Certification pathways
Transfer Cohort Bridge
8
Accelerated transfer progression
Pre-Health (Proposed)
5
Future MD / DO / PA / Pharmacist / DDS-DMD
Esports & Digital Arts
4
Emerging pathway

Design Philosophy — Why Specialized Badges Exist

Foundational badges measure core skill development. Milestone badges measure program-wide progression. Specialized badges measure partnership-specific depth — the credentials that signal a student has gone beyond the core into a high-value vertical where employers, graduate programs, and credentialing bodies recognize the work.

Every specialized badge is stacked: earning a specialized credential simultaneously progresses a student’s foundational track + milestone category. This stacking is the design principle that gives the 73-badge architecture its credential signal weight. A student earning Future CFO simultaneously advances Financial Literacy + Career Critical Thinker (foundational), Certification Achiever (milestone), and the Stuart C-Suite pathway (specialized) — three forms of evidence from one credential.

1. Stuart C-Suite Pathway — Black & Gold Series (5)

The highest-prestige badges in the Stuart ecosystem, representing cross-functional executive-level competency development. Each badge requires completing a curated sequence of activities that demonstrates readiness for a specific leadership trajectory. Two auto-earn triggers (CAPM → COO and SIE 70%+ → CFO) let industry certifications collapse into Elevate credentials.

BadgeRequirementsAuto-Earn TriggerFoundational + Milestone Stack
Future CEOAny Stuart Professional Certificate + Entrepreneurial Venture Development + Leadership L3+—Leadership L3 + Innovation Pioneer + Pro Comms L3
Future COOStuart Certificate + Leadership role in Stuart org + Collaboration L3+CAPM Certification (PMI)Collaboration L3 + Certification Achiever + Career Readiness L4
Future CFOStuart Certificate + Stuart Investments Membership + Financial LiteracySIE Exam score 70%+Financial Literacy + Critical Thinker L3 + Certification Achiever
Future CTOStuart Professional Certificate (ITM focus) + Tech+ project + ONE of: AWS Cert, Coding Project, ITM / Computing Org—Digital Literacy L4 + Tech+ Specialist + Certification Achiever
Future CMOAMA Membership + Professional Communications L3+ + Marketing Analytics Project—Pro Comms L3 + Digital Lit L3 + Industry Navigator

2. AWS Academy Pathway (17)

Free courseware + 50% exam discount on every AWS certification pathway. Elevate’s deepest external technical partnership and the most direct on-ramp into the cloud-computing labor market. 17 certification pathways span foundations, developing, architecting, security, AI/ML, data engineering, and operations.

#AWS PathwayHoursElevate Stack
1AWS Cloud Foundations~20 hrDigital Literacy L2 + Cloud Foundations milestone
2AWS Cloud Practitioner~15 hrCertification Achiever (milestone)
3AWS Cloud Developing~40 hrDigital Literacy L3
4AWS Developer Associate40–60 hrDigital Literacy L4–L5 + Certification Achiever
5AWS Cloud Architecting~40 hrDigital Literacy L4 + Future CTO pathway
6AWS Solutions Architect Associate40–60 hrFuture CTO anchor + Certification Achiever
7AWS Cloud Operations~40 hrDigital Literacy L3–L4
8AWS Operations Technician~30 hrDigital Literacy L3
9AWS Cloud Security Foundations~20 hrCybersecurity track + Digital Lit L3
10AWS Security Specialty40–60 hrCybersecurity + Certification Achiever
11AWS Security Engineering~40 hrCybersecurity + Certification Achiever
12AWS AI Foundations~20 hrAI Practitioner + Workshop 4
13AWS Generative AI~30 hrAI Practitioner L2 + Personal Brand Architect
14AWS Machine Learning Foundations~20 hrDigital Literacy L3 + Innovation Pioneer
15AWS NLP~25 hrAI Practitioner L3
16AWS Data Engineering~30 hrData Analytics track + Digital Lit L3
17AWS Data Center Tech~30 hrDigital Lit L3

3. Transfer Cohort Bridge Pathway (8)

Accelerated progression credentials for transfer students completing the one-year bridge program. Each badge surfaces the asset-based framing of Laanan’s Transfer Student Capital model and rewards transfers for the prior work and coursework that traditional credit-articulation systems often leave invisible.

BadgeEarned When
Prior Experience ChampionActivity 3 Prior Learning & Experience Audit completed with documented credit-equivalent prior coursework or work history.
Accelerated Career NavigatorTransfer student earns all 9 bridge activities + 2 electives within their bridge year.
Bridge BuilderBridge year completion + signed Transition Placement Form on file before the second academic year.
Rapid Integration SpecialistAll 9 bridge activities completed in ≤ 9 months (vs. 12-month standard bridge year).
Career Pivot StrategistWorkshop 13 + documented transferable-skills pivot narrative + new-field internship secured.
Transfer Peer MentorYear 2 transfer mentors an incoming transfer; documented mentor-meeting log.
Community-College Bridge ChampionTop-feeder community-college transfer (City Colleges of Chicago, College of DuPage, Moraine Valley, Triton) completes bridge year with 4+ Y1/Y2 credited via Activity 3.
L-TSQ Growth AchieverPre/post L-TSQ administration shows documented growth in transfer student capital across the bridge year.

4. Pre-Health Pathway (5 — Proposed)

Proposed badge track for Lewis College students on health-professional pathways. Each badge requires the specific application-readiness components that medical, dental, pharmacy, and PA admissions committees evaluate, with reflection requirements anchored to AAC&U Service Learning and CASEL Social Awareness.

BadgeTarget Pathway & Earning Requirements
Future MDMedical school pathway. Requirements: MCAT prep documented + 100+ clinical / shadowing hours + service learning + undergraduate research project with faculty sign-off.
Future DOOsteopathic medical pathway. Requirements: MCAT prep + osteopathic-principles shadowing + clinical hours + service / community-health reflection.
Future PAPhysician Assistant pathway. Requirements: documented healthcare experience hours + GRE / PA-CAT prep + patient-facing reflection.
Future PharmacistPharmD pathway. Requirements: pharmacy technician experience + PCAT prep + community-pharmacy service component.
Future DDS / DMDDental school pathway. Requirements: documented dental shadowing hours + DAT prep + research or service-learning component.

5. Esports & Digital Arts Pathway (4 — Emerging)

Emerging specialized track for students pursuing the rapidly growing gaming + esports + content-creation labor market. Anchored to NACE Technology, P21 Creativity, and the WEF Future of Jobs 2030 creative-industries skill set. The 1,211+ student Discord community is the recruitment funnel.

BadgeEarned When
Game Design FoundationsComplete 10–15 hr game-design project; portfolio artifact required (playable demo or design document).
Esports Event Production Specialist12–20 hr tournament-operations role; broadcasting or production responsibilities documented with role description and reflection.
Gaming Content Creator15–25 hr streamer / content-creator project + monetization or sponsorship documentation; cross-platform analytics required.
Esports Analytics Researcher15–25 hr analytics project with documented data analysis (team performance, viewership, market research); connects to Digital Lit L3.

How Specialized Badges Stack with Foundational + Milestone

Every specialized badge cross-walks to at least one foundational track + one milestone category. The credential signal is not one badge — it is three forms of evidence emerging from one set of work.

Specialized BadgeFoundational Tracks AdvancedMilestone Category Earned
Future CFO (Stuart)Financial Literacy, Career Critical ThinkerCertification Achiever
AWS Cloud ArchitectDigital Literacy L4Certification Achiever + Future CTO
Bridge Builder (Transfer)Self-Awareness, Career & Self-DevInitiative Taker + Resilience & Persistence Leader
Future MD (Pre-Health)Critical Thinker, Pro CommsResearch Scholar + Community Impact Leader
Esports Analytics ResearcherDigital Literacy L3Innovation Pioneer + Industry Navigator

Specialized-Badge Roadmap — What’s Next

Summer 2026

  • Stuart C-Suite auto-earn pipeline (CAPM & SIE 70%+ triggers) live on 312
  • AWS Academy spring exam cycle results & cert-issued badges
  • Transfer Cohort Bridge specialized badges (8) provisioned for Fall 2026 launch

Fall 2026

  • Pre-Health (5) badges piloted with Lewis College pre-health advising
  • Esports & Digital Arts (4) emerging-track formal launch
  • First batch of Stuart C-Suite Black & Gold badge earners recognized

2027

  • Quantum Computing specialized badge track (CQE-partnered) launched
  • Open Badges 3.0 / Verifiable Credentials issuance live for all specialized badges
  • Employer Verifier Endpoint deployed so recruiters can validate specialized credentials in real time

Framework Crosswalk

Thorough mapping of all 73 Elevate badges against the eight national and international career-readiness frameworks that underpin the Elevate evidence base.

Canonical badge count 73

The dashboard locks to 73 active and trackable badge types on the 312 platform as of May 10, 2026 (source: Elevate Master Reference Document, § 2). Two valid views of this number are presented across the dashboard. View A (organizational): 25 foundational (5 tracks × 5 levels) + 20 milestone (4 color categories) + 28 specialized (partnership tracks) = 73 — used on the Badge Catalog page. View B (topical, below): groups badges by content area to expose framework alignment patterns; the row totals add to the same 73. Any future additions update both views in lockstep.

Every Elevate badge is mapped to all eight foundational frameworks. Each cell reflects the strength of alignment a badge has to that framework’s competency set as validated by the badge’s verification standard (automatic / portfolio / external / peer). Cells are coded as Primary (badge directly credentials this framework’s anchor competency), Secondary (badge meaningfully reinforces it), or Supporting (badge touches it but it is not the primary construct).

Framework key: NACE · P21 · WEF 2030 · AAC&U HIPs · O*NET · CASEL · DOE ESF · Kolb ELT — defined below the matrix.

Badge NACE P21 WEF 2030 AAC&U HIPs O*NET CASEL DOE ESF Kolb ELT Primary Competency

The Eight Foundational Frameworks

Code Framework Construct credentialed Elevate coverage
NACENational Association of Colleges and Employers — 8 Career Readiness CompetenciesCareer & Self-Development, Communication, Critical Thinking, Equity & Inclusion, Leadership, Professionalism, Teamwork, Technology92%
P21Partnership for 21st Century Learning — 4 Cs + Literacies + Life & CareerCritical Thinking, Creativity, Communication, Collaboration; Information / Media / Tech literacies; Life & Career Skills95%
WEF 2030World Economic Forum Future of Jobs 2025 — Top-15 skills for 2025–2030Analytical Thinking, AI & Big Data, Tech Literacy, Active Learning, Resilience, Empathy, Curiosity, Leadership & Social Influence88%
AAC&U HIPsAssociation of American Colleges & Universities — 11 High-Impact PracticesFYE, Common Intellectual Experiences, Learning Communities, Writing-Intensive, Collaborative Projects, Undergraduate Research, Diversity / Global, Service Learning, Internships, Capstone, ePortfolios82%
O*NETU.S. Department of Labor Occupational Information Network12 core occupational skill categories: Basic, Complex Problem-Solving, Resource Mgmt, Social, Systems, Technical — mapped to 900+ occupations85%
CASELCollaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning — 5 SEL CompetenciesSelf-Awareness, Self-Management, Social Awareness, Relationship Skills, Responsible Decision-Making78%
DOE ESFU.S. Department of Education Employability Skills Framework3 categories × 11 sub-skills: Applied Knowledge, Effective Relationships, Workplace Skills75%
Kolb ELTKolb Experiential Learning Theory — the design backbone of the Elevate tier systemConcrete Experience → Reflective Observation → Abstract Conceptualization → Active ExperimentationFoundational

Source: Elevate Master Reference Document (May 2026), § 3 The Eight-Framework Foundation.

Proposed New Badges — Closing Framework Gaps

The current 73-badge catalog covers the historical core of the eight foundational frameworks. The expanded framework set (Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report, WEF 2025 Future of Jobs, AAMC Core Competencies, UN SDGs, OECD AI Principles, U.S. DOL Registered Apprenticeship standards, WCAG 2.2, Burning Glass Hybrid Job Economy) surfaces twelve specific gaps where Elevate today has no dedicated badge.

Each candidate below is intentionally non-redundant with the existing catalog — it credentials a competency that current badges do not already serve as their primary construct. Recommended sequencing pairs each candidate with the year-in-pathway where the activity portfolio is strongest.

Proposed Badge NACE P21 WEF 2030 AAC&U HIPs O*NET CASEL DOE ESF Kolb ELT Primary Competency Year Phase
1. Generative AI PractitionerTechnologyY2–Y3
Framework anchor: Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report (~866% YoY GenAI growth, 2024 ed.) · WEF 2025 AI & Big Data #2
Gap closed: Current AI & ML Explorer addresses ML literacy; this badge specifically credentials prompt engineering, GenAI ethics, AI-assisted workflows, and tool fluency (Claude / ChatGPT / Copilot / domain-specific assistants) — the largest single skill gap identified by Coursera 2024–2026.
2. Data Ethics & Responsible AI StewardEthics & ProfessionalismY2–Y3
Framework anchor: AAC&U Ethical Reasoning & Action ELO · OECD AI Principles · NACE Professionalism
Gap closed: No current badge credentials the responsibility side of the AI economy — bias auditing, model cards, FERPA/HIPAA-aware AI use, IRB readiness for AI-touching research. Distinct from technical AI work.
3. Sustainability & ESG AnalystSustainabilityY2–Y3
Framework anchor: UN SDGs · WEF 2025 Environmental Stewardship #6 · O*NET Green Economy taxonomy
Gap closed: Zero current Elevate badges credential sustainability literacy — a fast-growing employer ask across architecture, engineering, business, and pre-health. Anchors Illinois Tech to the green-economy hiring lane.
4. Civic Engagement & Public Service LeaderCivic EngagementY1–Y2
Framework anchor: AAC&U Civic Knowledge & Engagement HIP · CASEL Responsible Decision-Making · DOE ESF Personal Qualities
Gap closed: Community Impact & Engagement Leader rewards service delivery; this badge credentials civic literacy, policy advocacy, voter education, and the deliberative-democracy skill set explicitly named in AAC&U Essential Learning Outcomes.
5. Async & Remote Collaboration SpecialistTeamwork (Async)Y2–Y3
Framework anchor: WEF 2025 Distributed Work cluster · NACE Teamwork · DOE ESF Interpersonal Skills
Gap closed: Existing Collaboration tiers assume synchronous co-located teamwork. A distinct badge for written-first, time-zone-aware, async collaboration recognizes the operating model 70%+ of post-grad knowledge work now requires.
6. Quantitative Reasoning & Data Storytelling ExpertCritical Thinking / CommunicationY2–Y3
Framework anchor: P21 Quantitative Literacy · AAC&U Quantitative Literacy ELO · Coursera Data Analytics #2
Gap closed: Current Data Analytics Professional credits technical depth; this badge credentials the storytelling layer — visual communication, chart selection, executive-readable narratives — increasingly demanded across non-STEM majors.
7. Wellness & Mental-Health Self-Management ChampionSelf-Management (CASEL)Y1–Y2
Framework anchor: CASEL Self-Management · WEF 2025 Resilience #5 · DOE ESF Personal Qualities
Gap closed: Adaptability Expert and Persistence Champion address resilience after-the-fact. This badge credentials proactive self-management practices (sleep hygiene, focus protocols, peer support training) tied to retention and post-graduation outcomes.
8. Pre-Health Clinical Readiness SpecialistPre-Health ReadinessY1–Y2
Framework anchor: AAMC Core Competencies · NACE Professionalism · O*NET 29-1000 Healthcare cluster
Gap closed: Pre-Health Partnership is live; the Clinical Practicum Excellence badge rewards post-experience. This badge credentials pre-clinical fundamentals (HIPAA, sterile technique, patient-communication basics) and aligns to AAMC entry expectations.
9. Hackathon & Competitive Programming AchieverCritical Thinking / InnovationY1–Y4
Framework anchor: NACE Critical Thinking · WEF Analytical Thinking · CDIO standards
Gap closed: Hackathons & Competitions is a dedicated dashboard track but no badge currently credentials placement, prize wins, or sustained hackathon participation as a distinct experiential credential.
10. Open-Source ContributorTechnologyY2–Y4
Framework anchor: WEF Tech Literacy · O*NET Software Developers · NACE Technology
Gap closed: Cloud / AWS / Tech Pioneer badges credential certifications and proprietary platforms. Open-source contribution (verified PRs, maintainer-style engagement) is a distinct, employer-trusted signal — especially for CS, CIS, applied math.
11. Accessibility & Inclusive Design PractitionerEquity, Inclusion & DesignY2–Y3
Framework anchor: WCAG 2.2 · AAC&U Intercultural Knowledge ELO · Section 508 compliance
Gap closed: No current badge credentials a11y design — increasingly required across architecture, computing, and design-adjacent business roles. Bridges Equity & Inclusion with Technology categories.
12. Workforce Apprenticeship & Earn-While-You-Learn ScholarWorkforce ReadinessY2–Y4
Framework anchor: U.S. DOL Registered Apprenticeship standards · DOE ESF Workplace Skills · Burning Glass Hybrid Job Economy
Gap closed: Internship Excellence credentials supervised internships; this badge specifically recognizes apprenticeship-format work (paid, multi-semester, employer co-designed) — a fast-growing format aligned with the Burning Glass hybrid-skills hiring lane.

Implementation note

If the full proposed slate is adopted, the Elevate catalog moves from 73 to 85 badges — still a manageable 312 platform footprint and still within the recommended L1–L5 tier ceiling. Implementation costs are concentrated in design (artwork, color category, naming convention) and verification standard authoring; activity sourcing leverages existing partners (AWS, Stuart, Pre-Health, ELM, IPRO) for ten of twelve candidates.

Recommended decision points: (1) prioritize the GenAI Practitioner and Data Ethics & Responsible AI Steward pair for Fall 2026 to capture the Coursera-validated demand spike before competitor universities credential it; (2) sequence Sustainability & ESG Analyst, Civic Engagement & Public Service Leader, and Accessibility & Inclusive Design Practitioner together as the Spring 2027 “Responsibility Cohort” release for cross-school marketing alignment; (3) reserve Pre-Health Clinical Readiness Specialist for the Pre-Health Partnership cohort launch.

Frameworks: NACE = National Association of Colleges and Employers Career Readiness Competencies (2024–2026 ed.) | P21 = Partnership for 21st Century Learning | WEF 2030 = World Economic Forum Future of Jobs 2025 (skills horizon to 2030) | AAC&U HIPs = Association of American Colleges & Universities High-Impact Practices | O*NET = U.S. Department of Labor Occupational Information Network | CASEL = Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning | DOE ESF = U.S. Department of Education Employability Skills Framework | Kolb ELT = Kolb Experiential Learning Theory.

Top Skills by Framework

Comprehensive index of the top career-readiness and job skills drawn from every framework Elevate uses — each skill mapped to its Elevate badge track and to a citable source so executives, faculty, and employer partners can verify the construct against the original publication.

This index spans the full set of frameworks underpinning Elevate: NACE 8 Career Readiness Competencies, WEF Future of Jobs 2025 Top-15 Skills, P21 21st Century Learning, CASEL 5 SEL Competencies, AAC&U Essential Learning Outcomes, O*NET / U.S. Department of Labor occupational skills, the U.S. DOE Employability Skills Framework, the Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report top-10 priorities, and the Burning Glass / Lightcast Hybrid Job Economy taxonomy. Each row in the index below names the skill, identifies the source framework with its scholarly citation, and maps it to its corresponding Elevate badge or activity track.

Five Highlighted Skills — Scholarly Workforce-Development Definitions

The five anchor skills below carry the heaviest signal weight in current employer and labor-market research. Each is presented with a definition drawn directly from the source framework, plus a workforce-development application and the Elevate badge track that operationalizes it for Illinois Tech students.

NACE COMPETENCY

Communication

Definition (verbatim, NACE 2024). “Clearly and effectively exchange information, ideas, facts, and perspectives with persons inside and outside of an organization.”

Workforce-development application. NACE’s 2026 Spring Job Outlook (n=185) rates Communication at 98.7% importance but only 55.4% proficiency among recent graduates — the largest employer-perceived gap in the survey. The CAR Method (Challenge-Action-Result) is the most transferable workforce-development tool for closing this gap because it produces a single artifact that travels from resume bullet to interview answer to LinkedIn summary.

Elevate operationalization. Professional Communications track L1–L5; Workshops 2 (CAR), 5 (Informational Interviews), 9 (Personal Brand), 11 (Interview Prep).

Citation. National Association of Colleges and Employers. (2024). Career readiness competencies for college graduates. NACE. https://naceweb.org/career-readiness/competencies/

WEF #1 SKILL FOR 2025–2030

Analytical Thinking

Definition (verbatim, WEF 2025). “The ability to break down complex information or problems into parts, identify patterns or relationships, and draw evidence-based conclusions.”

Workforce-development application. Ranked the #1 priority skill for the 2025–2030 hiring window. The WEF Future of Jobs 2025 report identifies analytical thinking as the foundational competency that enables AI, data analytics, and complex problem-solving roles — the three highest-growth role families in the global labor market.

Elevate operationalization. Career Critical Thinker track L1–L5; Industry Research Deep Dive elective; Business Case Competitions; Workshop 13 (Career Pivoting); IPRO Innovation Day reflection rubric.

Citation. World Economic Forum. (2025). The future of jobs report 2025. Geneva, Switzerland. https://www.weforum.org/publications/the-future-of-jobs-report-2025/

CASEL CORE COMPETENCY

Self-Awareness

Definition (verbatim, CASEL 2023). “The abilities to understand one’s own emotions, thoughts, and values and how they influence behavior across contexts. This includes capacities to recognize one’s strengths and limitations with a well-grounded sense of confidence and purpose.”

Workforce-development application. Self-awareness is the precondition for every other career-development decision: choice of major, choice of internship, response to feedback, and approach to negotiation. The Purdue-Gallup Index (2014) found that graduates who identified a mentor who helped them “understand themselves” were 2.2× more likely to be engaged at work post-graduation.

Elevate operationalization. Self-Awareness & Growth foundational track (Self-Awareness Developer L1–L5); Co-Curricular Plan SMART-goal setting; the Framework-Based Self-Assessment on the Assessments page; Year 1 Elevate Success Coach Check-In.

Citation. Collaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning. (2023). CASEL’s SEL framework: What are the core competence areas and where are they promoted? https://casel.org/fundamentals-of-sel/what-is-the-casel-framework/

P21 4 Cs / 21ST CENTURY LEARNING

Critical Thinking & Problem Solving

Definition (verbatim, P21 / Battelle for Kids 2019). “Reason effectively using various types of reasoning (inductive, deductive, etc.) appropriate to the situation; use systems thinking; make judgments and decisions based on the best analysis; and solve different kinds of non-familiar problems in both conventional and innovative ways.”

Workforce-development application. The Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report cites Critical Thinking as a durable human skill that continues to grow in employer demand even as technical skills surge (+41% YoY growth in associated course enrollments). NACE rates Critical Thinking at 93.5% importance against 50.0% proficiency — the second-largest gap in the 2026 employer survey.

Elevate operationalization. Career Critical Thinker track L1–L5; Workshop 2 (CAR Method deep dive); Industry Research Deep Dive elective; Stuart Business Case Competition; the Watson-Glaser / Raven’s aptitude instruments under review (see Assessments → Pearson exploration).

Citation. Partnership for 21st Century Learning. (2019). Framework for 21st century learning definitions. Battelle for Kids. https://battelleforkids.org/networks/p21

COURSERA 2026 #1 PRIORITY SKILL

AI Literacy & Generative AI Fluency

Definition (synthesized, Coursera 2024–2026; NACE 2026; WEF 2025). The ability to identify and ethically apply AI tools to professional tasks, develop effective prompts to elicit quality outputs, critically analyze and revise AI-generated work, and use AI to increase work productivity. AI literacy includes both technical fluency and the judgment to know when AI use is appropriate and when it is not.

Workforce-development application. Coursera’s 2024 Job Skills Report cited a ~866% year-over-year growth in Generative AI course enrollments — the largest single skill-demand shift Coursera had measured. NACE 2026 finds 35% of entry-level jobs now require AI skills and 57.8% of employers assign interns AI projects. For the Class of 2026, AI literacy is no longer optional — it is foundational.

Elevate operationalization. AI Practitioner specialized badge track; Workshop 4 (Applying for Jobs with AI — Using AI Ethically); AWS AI Foundations + AWS Generative AI pathways; the AI Skills mini-assessment integrated into the Framework-Based Self-Assessment (Assessments page).

Citations. Coursera. (2024, 2025, 2026). Job Skills Reports. National Association of Colleges and Employers. (2026). Job Outlook 2026 (Spring update). NACE. World Economic Forum. (2025). The future of jobs report 2025.

Full Skills-by-Framework Index — All Frameworks

NACE 8 Career Readiness Competencies (Citation: NACE, 2024)

SkillNACE Definition (paraphrased)Elevate Badge Track
1. Career & Self-DevelopmentProactively develop oneself and one’s career through continual personal & professional learning, awareness of strengths and weaknesses, and networking.Initiative Taker (milestone) + Self-Awareness Developer (foundational)
2. CommunicationClearly and effectively exchange information, ideas, facts, and perspectives with persons inside and outside an organization.Professional Communications track L1–L5
3. Critical ThinkingIdentify and respond to needs based on understanding situational context and logical analysis of relevant information.Career Critical Thinker track L1–L5
4. Equity & InclusionDemonstrate the awareness, attitude, knowledge, and skills required to equitably engage and include people from different local and global cultures.Cultural Bridge Builder + Global Citizen
5. LeadershipRecognize and capitalize on personal and team strengths to achieve organizational goals.Leadership Development track L1–L5
6. ProfessionalismKnowing work environments differ greatly, demonstrate understanding of workplace cultures, follow norms, take responsibility, manage time and tasks.Career Readiness L3+ + Industry Navigator
7. TeamworkBuild and maintain collaborative relationships to work effectively toward common goals.Collaboration Champion track L1–L5
8. TechnologyUnderstand and leverage technologies ethically to enhance efficiencies, complete tasks, and accomplish goals.Digital Literacy Champion track L1–L5

Citation: National Association of Colleges and Employers. (2024). Career readiness competencies for college graduates. NACE. https://naceweb.org/career-readiness/competencies/

How to Cite a Skill from This Page

For executive briefings: Use the in-line source attribution (e.g., “Analytical Thinking is the #1 priority skill for 2025–2030 per the World Economic Forum’s Future of Jobs Report 2025”).

For accreditation, grant writing, or scholarly use: Cite directly from the source framework using the APA 7 citations provided beneath each table. The Elevate dashboard’s comprehensive reference list is maintained at elevateillinoistech.github.io/ElevateDashboard0226/ and in the Framework Alignment page’s reference list.

For employer-facing communication: Pair the framework citation with the Elevate badge track in a single line — e.g., “Critical Thinking (NACE 2024, 93.5% importance) — verified via the Elevate Career Critical Thinker L1–L5 badge progression.” This pairing lets recruiters validate the construct externally while seeing how Illinois Tech operationalizes it.

Badge Year 1 Activities Year 2 Activities
Digital Literacy Champion Track

Digital Literacy L4 — AWS Cloud Skills (Advanced)

Communications L2 Career Fair Prep, Hawk Ambassador LinkedIn Profile, Prof Dev Workshop
Communications L3 — Resume Review, Mock Interview, Networking Event
Career Critical Thinker Track

Critical Thinking L2 IPRO Innovation Day Resume Review, Prof Dev Workshop
Critical Thinking L3 — Industry Research, Mock Interview, Informational Interviews
Leadership Development Track

Leadership L2 ELM Leadership, Hawk Ambassador Hawks in Action - Athletics

Collaboration Champion Track

Collaboration L2 Community Service Day, Hawks in Action —
Collaboration L3 — Hawks in Action - Athletics
Milestone & Achievement Badges

Experience Collector — Sophomore Elevate Experience (10+ Hours)
Global Citizen Chicago Adventures, International Immersion International Cultural Immersion
Initiative Taker Find Your Flock - Student Org Student Organization Leadership
Professional Relationship Builder Elevate Mentorship Programs Industry Networking Event, Informational Interviews
Industry Navigator Career Fair Prep Squad Industry Research Deep Dive
Innovation Catalyst IPRO Innovation Day Professional Portfolio Development
Technology Pioneer AWS Cloud Skills AWS Cloud Skills (Advanced)
Adaptability Expert International Cultural Immersion International Cultural Immersion

Persistence Champion Hope Chicago & Chicago Difference —
ELM Practitioner ELM Leadership Program —
Organizational Leadership Executive — Student Organization Leadership

Note: Each activity awards 1-3 badges. Wild Card experiences (Elevator Pitch, Flight Plan) map to relevant skill tracks based on the experience type. Year 2 activities generally award higher-level badges (L2-L4) than Year 1 activities (L1-L2).
