Current State — What Athletes Already Earn

Hawks Beyond the Field (existing Elevate activity)

What it credentials. Participation in Illinois Tech varsity sports, club sports, intramurals, rec leagues, cultural sports (cricket, martial arts, dragon boat racing, table tennis, soccer leagues, etc.), and volunteer coaching in the Chicago community.

Badges currently earned by athletes through participation:

  • Team Player (Collaboration L1–L2 anchor)
  • Time Management (Self-Awareness Developer / Persistence Champion anchor)
  • Discipline & Commitment (Resilience & Flexibility anchor)
  • Collaboration (foundational track, L1–L5)
  • Leadership Development (foundational track, L1–L5 — team captains and officers earn additional credit)
  • Community Champion (Community Impact & Engagement Leader anchor)

Gap. The language inside 312 and on student-facing channels does not yet explicitly speak to athletes. The Summer 2026 expansion makes the credentialing visible, automated where possible, and discoverable from the Athletics website.

Three Participation Levels — Equitable Recognition

Varsity (NCAA Division III)

Illinois Tech Scarlet Hawks compete at the NCAA D-III level. Season completion, captaincy, conference / national recognition, athletic milestones map to the highest-tier athletics badges.

Club Sports

Student-led competitive teams (e.g., esports, table tennis, soccer leagues, fencing). Captains, officers, treasurers, and event organizers earn enhanced leadership credit.

Intramural & Rec Leagues

Open campus rec leagues across basketball, soccer, volleyball, flag football. Lowest barrier-to-entry tier; full-season participation earns Team Player badge.

Cultural Sports (international students)

Cricket, martial arts, dragon boat racing, table tennis leagues, and other heritage-anchored sports. Equity-by-design: international students get the same credentialing weight as varsity athletes.

Volunteer Coaching

Coaching in the Chicago community (youth leagues, AAU, school programs). Counts as Community Champion + Leadership track.

5 Proposed New Athletics Activities — Summer 2026

Each activity is drafted from the Mar–Apr 2026 email scope between Fred Logan and Mark Johnston. Hours are typical commitments; verification standard sits within Elevate’s four-method framework (Automatic, Portfolio, External, Peer).

#ActivityHoursVerificationBadge Targets
1Co-Branded “Elevate Athletics” Season CompletionFull season (60–200 hr)Automatic — roster + season-end coach sign-offStudent-Athlete Season Completer (NEW); Team Player; Collaboration L2–L3; Leadership L2 (if captain)
2Captain & Officer Leadership CreditRole-long (30–90 hr)External — coach / advisor confirmation of roleTeam Captain & Leadership Catalyst (NEW); Leadership L3–L4; Org Leadership Executive
3Career Workshops Delivered TO Athletes5–10 hr / workshop seriesAutomatic — attendance + post-workshop reflectionPro Comms L2–L3; Industry Navigator; Athlete Career Translator (NEW)
4Athlete Career Outcomes Tracking2–3 hr (survey + interview)Portfolio — first-destination survey + alumni follow-upCareer Outcomes Contributor (NEW); Self-Awareness Developer; Industry Navigator
5Cultural & International Sports RecognitionVariable (season-based)Peer — league captain / cultural org president sign-off + reflectionGlobal Citizen; Cultural Intelligence Leader; Community Champion; Team Player

Proposed Badge NACE P21 WEF 2030 AAC&U HIPs O*NET CASEL DOE ESF Kolb ELT Primary Competency
1. Student-Athlete Season Completer Teamwork & Resilience

Primary alignment    Secondary alignment    Supporting alignment

Badge Definitions & Verification Standards

1. Student-Athlete Season Completer

Awarded for completing a full season in any of the four participation levels (Varsity / Club / Intramural / Cultural). Auto-trigger via Athletics roster + coach end-of-season confirmation.

Tier: L2 · Verification: Automatic + External

2. Team Captain & Leadership Catalyst

Awarded to team captains, club officers, and rec-league commissioners. Includes a brief written reflection (300 words) on a real leadership moment from the season + a coach / advisor sign-off.

Tier: L3 · Verification: External + Portfolio

3. Athlete Career Translator

Awarded after completing the Career Services “Translate Your Game” workshop series (resume language for athletes, LinkedIn for athletes, mock interview with athletic-context examples) + producing an artifact (resume + LinkedIn).

Tier: L2–L3 · Verification: Automatic + Portfolio

4. Career Outcomes Contributor (athletes)

Awarded to graduating student-athletes who complete the first-destination survey + a short alumni-follow-up interview. Feeds the longitudinal outcomes pipeline Mark explicitly asked for.

Tier: L2 · Verification: Portfolio

Student Government Association (SGA) Partnership

Credentialing civic engagement, governance literacy, and student-leadership work that Illinois Tech’s elected and appointed student leaders already perform — built in collaboration with the SGA executive board and the Office of Student Affairs.

Early stage · June 2026 co-design working session proposed
“

SGA officers, senators, and committee members already do leadership work — Elevate just isn’t credentialing it visibly. The partnership has moved from cold-intro to active discovery.

Where we are (May 2026): SGA was introduced to Elevate by Mallik. Dr. Logan ran a full platform walkthrough for the SGA leadership team. Delwin Manjali (SGA) confirmed SGA will champion Elevate to the student body, share feedback as students engage with the platform, and explore collaborative fall events. SGA elections are wrapping up in parallel.

Proposed next step: a June 2026 working session to co-design SGA-specific Elevate activities and badges — pilot only, scoped to SGA participation. Anything on this page below is a working draft, intended to seed the June conversation rather than predetermine it. Final activities and badges will be defined collaboratively with SGA before any Fall 2026 pilot.

June 2026
Co-design Working Session Proposed
SGA + Career Services activity / badge design
5
Existing Elevate Badges SGA Members Already Earn
Leadership L3–L4, Org Leadership Executive, Pro Comms, Critical Thinker, Community Champion
3
Governance Tiers Credentialed
Executive Board · Senator · Committee Member
4 · 3
Working-Draft Activities · Working-Draft Badges
Seed material for the June session — not finalized
Fall 2026
Pilot Target
Pending June co-design + Exec Board sign-off

Why This Partnership Matters

Civic engagement is undercredentialed in higher education. AAC&U’s Essential Learning Outcomes name Civic Knowledge & Engagement as a core competency, but most institutions don’t credential the actual work students do in student-government roles. Illinois Tech’s Elevate framework is unusually well-positioned to fix this because the architecture already supports stacked credentials: an SGA officer simultaneously progresses Leadership L3–L4, Org Leadership Executive (milestone), and the proposed Civic Engagement & Public Service Leader badge from a single year of service.

Employer signal. Per the NACE 2026 Job Outlook (Spring Update, n=185), Leadership ranks among the top-10 most-sought resume attributes for the Class of 2026, and the gap between employer-rated importance and observed proficiency is −10.7 points. SGA service is one of the most defensible, evidence-rich ways for a student to demonstrate Leadership and Professionalism competencies to a recruiter — provided the institution credentials the work.

Partnership Leads

SGA Primary Contact

Delwin Manjali · SGA leadership
Confirmed SGA will champion Elevate to the student body and share student-facing feedback. Coordinating the June 2026 co-design working session and the fall collaborative-event slate.

Introducer / Internal Sponsor

Mallik made the SGA introduction after Dr. Logan’s overview to SGA leadership, recommending a deeper walkthrough with the full group. Continues as cross-org connector.

SGA Exec Board & Advisor

Incoming AY 2026–27 SGA Exec Board (post-election) + SGA Advisor / Office of Student Affairs. To be looped in for the June working session; SGA leads will extend the invite to colleagues whose insight is most relevant to the activity / badge design.

Career Services Owner

Dr. Freddrick Logan, PhD · Experiential Learning Manager
flogan1@illinoistech.edu · MTCC Suite 106

NowDr. Logan proposed a June 2026 collaborative working session to co-design SGA-specific Elevate activities and digital badges as a pilot.Awaiting SGA to book a June time; SGA invited to extend the invite to relevant SGA colleagues.

Cadence note: SGA elections are concluding this term. The June co-design session intentionally bridges the outgoing and incoming SGA leadership so both cohorts shape the pilot design.

Three Governance Tiers — Equitable Recognition

Tier 1 · Executive Board

President, Vice President, Treasurer, Secretary, Chief of Staff. Year-long term. Highest-tier credentialing — targets Leadership L4–L5 + Organizational Leadership Executive + proposed SGA Officer milestone badge.

Tier 2 · Senators & Committee Chairs

Elected senators representing colleges, schools, or class years. Chairs of standing committees (academic affairs, finance, student life, diversity & inclusion). Targets Leadership L3 + Critical Thinker L3 + Civic Engagement (proposed).

Tier 3 · Committee Members & Volunteers

Appointed committee members; SGA-event volunteers; student-government interns. Lowest barrier-to-entry; targets Leadership L2 + Community Champion + Collaboration L2.

Current State — Badges SGA Members Already Earn

SGA service already maps to multiple Elevate badges through existing activities — the partnership doesn’t require new tracks, it just makes the credentialing visible and explicit to SGA participants:

  • Organizational Leadership Executive (milestone) — SGA Executive Board members; chairs.
  • Leadership L3–L4 (foundational) — senators and committee chairs.
  • Professional Communications L3 — speaking at senate meetings, writing policy memos, presenting to the Office of the Provost.
  • Critical Thinker L3 — budget review, policy analysis, resolution drafting.
  • Community Impact & Engagement Leader — SGA-led campus initiatives and Chicago-community service projects.

4 Working-Draft SGA-Specific Activities For June co-design

Seed material for the June 2026 working session. SGA may add, remove, reshape, or replace any of these — the goal is co-designed activities that reflect what SGA participation actually looks like at Illinois Tech.

#ActivityHoursVerificationBadge Targets
1SGA Term of Service (Officer / Senator / Chair)Year-long (~80–120 hr)External — SGA Advisor confirmation of role + end-of-term reflectionSGA Officer milestone (NEW); Org Leadership Executive; Leadership L3–L4
2Policy Memo or Resolution Authoring8–15 hrPortfolio — authored memo / resolution + SGA-vote outcomeCivic Engagement & Public Service Leader (proposed); Pro Comms L3; Critical Thinker L3
3Student Voter / Civic Literacy Campaign15–25 hrPortfolio — campaign deliverables (registration drives, voter-info events, debate watch parties)Civic Engagement (proposed); Community Impact Leader; Pro Comms L2–L3
4SGA Committee Service (semester-long)20–40 hr / semesterExternal — committee chair confirmation; participation log; output artifactLeadership L2–L3; Collaboration L2–L3; Committee Service milestone (NEW)

3 Working-Draft SGA Badges — Full 8-Framework Alignment For June co-design

Working-draft badge concepts mapped to all eight foundational frameworks — included to show how SGA-centric badges would slot into the existing crosswalk without disrupting current architecture. Final badge set is the output of the June co-design session, not an input. Coding: P = Primary, S = Secondary, U = Supporting, — = not applicable.

Proposed Badge NACE P21 WEF 2030 AAC&U HIPs O*NET CASEL DOE ESF Kolb ELT Primary Competency
1. SGA Officer / Senator Leadership & Governance
2. Civic Engagement & Public Service Leader Civic Engagement
3. SGA Committee Service Collaboration & Service

Primary alignment    Secondary alignment    Supporting alignment

Badge Definitions & Verification Standards

1. SGA Officer / Senator

Awarded for completing a full year of elected service on the SGA Executive Board or as a senator. SGA Advisor confirmation + 500-word end-of-term reflection on a real governance moment (resolution authored, budget reviewed, conflict mediated).

Tier: L3–L4 · Verification: External + Portfolio

2. Civic Engagement & Public Service Leader

Awarded for sustained civic-engagement work (policy advocacy, voter education, civic-literacy campaigns, public-service projects). Distinguished from Community Impact Leader by its policy-and-advocacy dimension. Aligns with AAC&U Civic Knowledge & Engagement HIP.

Tier: L3 · Verification: Portfolio + External

3. SGA Committee Service

Awarded for semester-long sustained service on an SGA standing committee. Committee-chair confirmation + participation log + at least one tangible deliverable (committee report, recommendation, event executed).

Tier: L2–L3 · Verification: External + Portfolio

Employer Engagement Tiers

Tiered structure that lets employers engage at the scale that matches their hiring volume, brand strategy, and Elevate-aligned recruiting goals.

TierEngagementTypical HostsElevate Value-Add
Tier 1 · AnchorMulti-year strategic partnership: curriculum, hiring pipeline, sponsorship, co-branded credentialsAWS, Microsoft, Accenture, SalesforceFuture CTO/CMO trigger badges; cohort sponsorship; dashboard partner spotlight
Tier 2 · Strategic RecruitingAnnual recruiting cadence + sponsored events~10–20 firms across Chicago tech, finance, healthcare, consultingFiltered badge-verified candidate lists; Stuart business networking presence

Tier 4 · Project-Based HostHosts IPRO, capstone, virtual internship, or hackathon teamsChicago architecture firms (SOM, Gensler, Studio Gang); Chicago Innovation Ecosystem (1871, mHUB, Founders Institute)Innovation Pioneer badge attribution; Stuart Tech+ integration

Employer Badge Dashboard — Verifiable Talent Pipeline

Open Badges 3.0 + W3C Verifiable Credentials let recruiters filter candidates by earned badges. Each badge surfaces the underlying activity, hours invested, verification method, and aligned framework (NACE / P21 / WEF / etc.) — turning the credential into a transparent skill record, not a checkbox.

Roadmap. Integrates with the long-range Illinois Tech Talent platform (AI-driven, institution-only matching). Future-state aligned with the MIT Digital Credentials Consortium (see CLI page) so employer verification works across vendor boundaries.

Annual Recruiting Calendar

WindowAnchor EventEmployer Touch-Point
Aug – SepFind Your Flock / Y1 OrientationAnchor partners host sponsorship sessions and brand activations
OctoberFall Career & Internship Fair~110 companies; Career Fair Strategist badge attribution
Nov – DecIndustry info-sessions + Stuart Business NetworkingMonthly cadence; Tier 1/2 partner brand visits
Jan – FebAWS Game Day + cohort kick-offsAnchor AWS partner; cert-pathway momentum
MarchSpring Career Fair130 companies; the largest employer touch-point of the year
Apr – MayIPRO Innovation Day + capstone showcasesTier 4 project-based host engagement; Innovation Pioneer attribution
SummerInternship season + Virtual InternshipsForage / Riipen platform; Industry Navigator + Experience Collector

How Employers Engage with Elevate

Recruit from badge-verified pools

Filter Handshake / Elevate candidate lists by earned badges (e.g., AWS Cloud Foundations + Pro Comms L3 + Critical Thinker L3). Replaces single-keyword resume search.

Sponsor a cohort

Underwrite an AWS Academy cohort, a Stuart C-Suite track, or the Pre-Health Clinical Readiness cohort. Branded co-curricular impact + early-pipeline access.

Host an IPRO / capstone project

Sponsor a real-world project for cross-disciplinary teams. Students earn Innovation Pioneer + Critical Thinker L4; employers get deliverables + recruiting exposure.

Donate Faculty Innovation Fund

Per the August 2025 directive: $1.5K–$25K Trustee-funded per faculty experience. Employer-sponsored faculty experiences are co-branded.

Host a Workshop Series session

Deliver a Career Services workshop (Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness, Salary Negotiation, AI & Productivity, etc.) to Y2 / Y3 Elevate cohorts.

Alumni Mentor Network

Activate company alumni as Elevate mentors for Y3 + Y4 students. 80,000+ Illinois Tech alumni in 130+ countries (see Mentorship page for the 10 global activation ideas).

Why Hire from Elevate — the Employer Value Proposition

Elevate graduates arrive with a verifiable, framework-aligned skill record — not just a transcript. Every credential traces back to demonstrated competency in NACE, P21, WEF 2030, AAC&U HIPs, O*NET, CASEL, DOE ESF, and Kolb ELT terms. Employers can verify the work, see the artifacts, and trust the signal.

  • The Elevate Guarantee — institutional commitment to student employment within six months of graduation. Employers hire from a pool with self-selected commitment.
  • Stacked credentials — one project earns foundational + milestone + specialized badges simultaneously. More evidence per hire.
  • 398 currently enrolled with 73 active badge types and a verified Workshop Series + Coaching Operations layer behind every credential.
  • Future-state DCC alignment means Elevate credentials remain verifiable independent of vendor platform — protecting the long-term value of every hire.

Employer Partnership Contacts

Career Services & Experiential Learning. BJ Engelhardt (Executive Director) · Nicholas Mancari (Director, Career Development & Engagement) · Dr. Freddrick Logan, PhD (Experiential Learning Manager — Elevate / Workforce Hybrid Platform Product).

Partnership inquiries. flogan1@illinoistech.edu · MTCC Suite 106 · 312.567.6800.

Virtual Internships Partnership

Illinois Tech’s guaranteed-placement global remote internship program — powered by the Virtual Internships platform. 14,000+ host companies across 80+ countries; counts toward the Elevate experience requirement. Live and accepting applications for Spring 2026.

Live program · Applying for Spring 2026
“

Illinois Tech students can gain global work experience from anywhere. All you need is a laptop and an internet connection.

The Virtual Internships platform connects Illinois Tech students with approved host companies worldwide — from startups to organizations like Facebook, Deutsche Bank, and Carrefour. Students choose their career field, weekly hours, and schedule; a dedicated 1:1 coach supports them start to finish. Completion satisfies the Elevate experience requirement and produces a curated project plan, program-completion certification, and a detailed performance evaluation. Live page: elevate.iit.edu/experiences

14,000+
Approved Host Companies
Startups through Fortune-500 firms worldwide
80+
Countries
Genuinely global host network
18
Career Fields
Tech, finance, marketing, design, engineering, and more
10–20
Hours / Week (10–12 weeks)
Flexible: 10, 15, or 20 hrs/week around coursework
1 in 4
Hired After
Conversion to offer post-internship

Why This Partnership Matters

Guaranteed placement. Unlike traditional internships where students compete for limited spots, every approved Illinois Tech applicant is matched with a host company. This eliminates the most common reason students don’t complete internships — they couldn’t land one.

Removes the equity bottleneck. No relocation costs, no travel, no visa complications, no housing. Works for commuter students, international students on F-1 visas, transfers, working students, and global learners pursuing Illinois Tech credentials online. 10–20 hrs/week for 10–12 weeks fits around full coursework.

Strategic fit with the three-population mandate. Virtual Internships simultaneously serves on-campus students, U.S. learners returning for the credential, and global learners across 100+ countries — the most direct embodiment of Illinois Tech’s international positioning.

Employer signal preserved. Per NACE 2026 Job Outlook, “internship or relevant experience” is a top-tier resume attribute. Virtual Internships engagements run with real host companies (Facebook, Deutsche Bank, Carrefour, and 14,000+ others) and produce a curated project plan, program-completion certification, and a detailed performance evaluation that goes onto the resume and into the Elevate badge dashboard.

Student Story
“

This opportunity gave me an advantage over other students when applying for future internships and jobs. It gave me invaluable insight into the world of work and increased my competence within my chosen field.

Eridon Keta · Product Development Intern at ARYZE, Denmark

Why Virtual Internships — Six Differentiators

Guaranteed Placement

No competing for spots. Enroll and you’re matched with a host company.

Flexible Schedule

10, 15, or 20 hrs/week for 10–12 weeks. Designed to fit around full coursework.

No Relocation Costs

Work from anywhere. No travel, housing, or visa issues for any student profile.

Global Network

Companies in 80+ countries across 18 career fields. Build connections across continents.

12+ Dev Courses Included

Professional development modules tailored to each placement build workplace skills before and during the internship.

Dedicated 1:1 Coach

A dedicated Virtual Internships coach supports each student start to finish — on top of the Elevate Success Coach assigned by Career Services.

How It Works

STEP 01

Discover

Enroll online via the Elevate experiences portal. Explore opportunities across 80+ countries and 18 career fields. Choose your weekly hours and target placement window.

STEP 02

Match & Upskill

Get matched with an approved host company. Complete tailored professional-development courses to build workplace readiness before placement begins.

STEP 03

Gain Experience

Complete the placement with 1:1 coach support. Earn program-completion certification, a detailed employer performance evaluation, and the Elevate experience credit.

Partnership Leads

Virtual Internships (platform)

Global remote internship platform. Approved host network of 14,000+ companies; dedicated 1:1 coach per student; 12+ professional-development courses; guaranteed placement model.

Illinois Tech Career Services

Owns student-facing intake, Elevate experience-requirement integration, Year 1 / Year 2 Success Coach handoff, and the elevate.iit.edu experiences portal.

Office of Global Services

Coordinates with international students on F-1 visa · CPT / OPT case-by-case eligibility review for virtual placements involving paid components.

Career Services Owner

Commuter Students & Remote Work Readiness

Mobile-first, asynchronous activity design for the commuter population — extended into a full Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness expansion track.

107
Year 2 Commuter Students
99% of 108 Y2 enrolled (May 2026 tracker)
10
Custom Commuter Activities (Jan 2026 proposal)
17
Existing Fully-Async Electives
30+
Total Virtual / Async-Eligible Options
4
Proposed New Remote-Work Badges

Why Commuter-First Design Matters

99% of Year 2 Elevate students are commuters (Elevate Master Reference Document, May 10 2026). Many work part-time, manage family responsibilities, or live 30–90 minutes from the Mies campus. Activities that require synchronous on-campus presence systematically disadvantage this population. The Commuter-Friendly track exists to ensure equitable badge progression regardless of physical proximity.

Design principles in force: mobile-first UX · asynchronous-eligible · minimal on-campus presence required · evidence captured through artifact uploads · time-flexible (10 hours over a term — not 10 hours in one event) · tangible-output required · mobile-tested forms.

The 10 Custom Commuter Activities — January 2026 Proposal

Bespoke async activities engineered from the Commuter-Friendly Activities Proposal (Jan 2026). All pass the seven design-principle tests above. Hours assume term-length completion (10–14 weeks).

#ActivityHoursArtifact / EvidenceBadge Targets
1Industry Podcast Learning Series6–10 hr5+ podcasts, 8 weekly summariesCritical Thinker L1, Pro Comms L1
2Virtual Informational Interviews (2+)4–8 hrZoom/phone interview + reflectionProfessional Relationship Builder, Pro Comms L2
3Free Industry Certification10–40 hrGoogle / HubSpot / LinkedIn Learning / Coursera certCertification Achiever, Digital Lit L2–L3
4AI Tools for Productivity5–8 hr5 AI-tool tutorial / demo deliverableAI & ML Explorer, Digital Lit L2, GenAI Practitioner (proposed)
5Digital Portfolio Development8–15 hrGitHub Pages / Notion / Wix portfolio — 5+ artifactsInnovation Pioneer, Digital Lit L3, Pro Comms L3
6Commute-to-Career Journal8–12 hr30-day mobile journal (5-min voice/text)Self-Awareness Developer, Persistence Champion
7LinkedIn Engagement Sprint5–8 hrProfile build + 10 thoughtful comments + 3 postsSocial Capital Specialist, Digital Lit L2, Pro Comms L2
8Async Mentor Conversation Series6–10 hr3 async Slack/Loom exchanges with mentor + summaryProfessional Relationship Builder, Pro Comms L2
9Self-Paced Coursera Specialization15–40 hrSpecialization completion certificateCertification Achiever, Critical Thinker L2–L3
10Forage / Riipen Virtual Job Simulation5–15 hrSimulation certificate + reflectionIndustry Navigator, Job Search Portfolio Strategist

Proposed Expansion · Fall 2026

Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness Track

A structured expansion of the Commuter-Friendly track that prepares Elevate students for distributed and remote-first careers — the operating model that 57% of college-educated workers currently engage with (Pew Research, 2024) and that captures a disproportionate share of post-graduation knowledge-economy roles. Built on the Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness workshop proposed in the Workshop Planning & Strategy session (May 2026).

Workshop Core — Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness

Anchor workshop sequenced as Y2 / Y3 elective and as a co-curricular module attached to internship preparation. Pulled directly from the workshop topics the team proposed during the Workshop Planning & Strategy session.

Module 1 · Remote Work Etiquette

Video-on conventions, response-time norms, written-first communication, meeting hygiene, distraction discipline.

Module 2 · Virtual Communication Tools

Slack / Teams / Zoom / Loom / Notion / Linear / Figma — tool-fit, workflow patterns, channel hygiene.

Module 3 · Async vs Sync Collaboration

When to use which mode · written-first norms · time-zone respect · documenting decisions.

Module 4 · Self-Management at Distance

Focus protocols, calendar discipline, burnout signals, manager-of-one mindset, async-status updates.

Module 5 · Home Office & Ergonomics

Workstation setup, lighting, audio, internet redundancy, distraction control, separation of work / personal space.

Module 6 · Applying for Remote Jobs

Remote-only job boards, search syntax, signal-vs-noise filtering, application channel strategy, references.

Skills & Education Required for Remote Work

Employer-validated skill set surfaced by the WEF 2025 Future of Jobs Report (distributed-work cluster), the Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report, and Buffer’s 2024 State of Remote Work survey (n=3,000+ remote workers across 100 countries).

Skill ClusterConstructElevate Operationalization
Written communicationClear, concise, written-first conventions; doc-first decisions; Slack / Notion / email fluencyPro Comms L3–L5; Workshop 5 (Cover-letter +); Async Mentor activity
Async collaborationTime-zone-aware planning, documenting context, reducing meeting loadAsync & Remote Collaboration Specialist (proposed badge); Collaboration L3–L5
Digital tooling fluencySlack, Teams, Zoom, Loom, Notion, Linear, Asana, Figma, GitHub, Google WorkspaceDigital Lit L2–L4; AI Tools for Productivity activity
Self-managementFocus protocols, calendar discipline, manager-of-one mindset, burnout preventionSelf-Awareness Developer; Wellness & Self-Management Champion (proposed)
Cybersecurity & data privacyVPN, password hygiene, phishing recognition, secure file sharing, BYOD policyCybersecurity Awareness Champion; Digital Lit L3
Personal-finance + tax literacyMulti-state filing, 1099 vs W-2, home-office deduction, retirement vehicles for remote workersFinancial Readiness Strategist; new Remote Worker Tax & Compliance module (proposed)
Cross-cultural fluencyWorking with distributed international teams, language-of-business considerations, holiday calendarsGlobal Citizen, Cultural Intelligence Leader, Intl Experience tracks
AI-augmented productivityGenAI for drafting, summarization, code-completion, meeting-notes; prompt fluencyAI & ML Explorer; GenAI Practitioner (proposed); AI Tools for Productivity activity

Tailoring Resume, Portfolio, and LinkedIn for Remote Work

Resume tailoring for remote

  • Highlight written-first achievements (docs, RFCs, async leadership)
  • Quantify async outcomes (PRs merged, docs published, async-collab artifacts)
  • Call out tools by name (Slack / Notion / Linear / GitHub)
  • Add a Remote-Ready Skills line under Skills
  • Surface location flexibility / time-zone willingness

Portfolio for remote roles

  • GitHub for code, GitHub Pages or Notion for narrative
  • Loom walk-throughs of artifacts (5–7 min each)
  • Written case studies — problem, decision, outcome, what I’d do differently
  • Public-by-default Slack / Discord community contributions
  • Verifiable contribution graph (commits, comments, RFCs)

LinkedIn for remote

  • “Open to Remote” toggle + headline including Remote-First
  • About section: time zone, work-from-anywhere readiness
  • Recommendations from async colleagues
  • Featured Loom intro video (60–90 sec)
  • Connect with remote-first companies (GitLab, Zapier, Doist)

Cover letter pattern

  • Lead with async-collab artifact (link, not claim)
  • Name the time-zone-overlap window
  • Reference a specific remote-culture company value
  • Close with a Loom link offering more context

Tax & Financial Logistics for Remote Workers

Why this matters: A remote-first first job often crosses tax jurisdictions in ways students didn’t encounter in summer internships. Elevate provides framework-level literacy — not legal advice — so students know which questions to bring to a qualified CPA or tax-prep service.

Out-of-scope: Individual tax preparation, legal interpretation of nexus rules, and immigration advisory. Elevate references qualified resources (university tax-clinic referrals, IRS Publication 519 for international students, Sprintax for non-residents).

TopicWhat students should knowAuthoritative reference
Multi-state filingWorking remotely from one state for an employer in another may create filings in both. Convenience-of-employer rules (NY, CT, DE, NE, PA) are a common surprise.IRS Pub 17; state-specific DOR sites
W-2 vs 1099 (contractor)Many remote-friendly roles are contractor positions — quarterly estimated taxes, self-employment tax, no employer-paid benefits.IRS Pub 334; Form 1040-ES
Home-office deductionAvailable to self-employed; generally not to W-2 employees post-TCJA. Simplified vs actual-expense method.IRS Pub 587
International (non-resident)F-1 / J-1 status implications, FICA exemption windows, treaty benefits, SSN vs ITIN.IRS Pub 519; Sprintax; OISA referrals
Retirement vehicles for remoteSEP-IRA, Solo 401(k), Roth IRA — what remote contractors should set up in Year 1 of full-time remote.IRS Pub 590-A; SEC investor.gov
State residency & nexus183-day rule, statutory residency vs domicile, snowbirding implications. Critical for digital-nomad-adjacent workers.State DOR residency guides

Digital Nomad Visas — Country Programs (2024–2026)

Many countries now offer remote-work / digital-nomad visa categories distinct from tourist or work visas. Income thresholds are typical eligibility gates and change frequently — figures below are illustrative and should be verified with consular sources before action. Reference table only; international advisory remains with the appropriate consulate or licensed counsel.

CountryVisa nameTypical durationIncome gate (typical)Notes
PortugalD8 Digital Nomad Visa1 yr renewable to 5~€3,280 / moPathway to residency; tax-favorable NHR (under reform)
SpainDigital Nomad Visa (Startups Law)1–3 yr renewable to 5~€2,650 / moBeckham Law tax option (24% flat for up to 6 yr)
EstoniaDigital Nomad VisaUp to 1 yr~€4,500 / moFirst country to formalize a digital-nomad visa (2020)
GermanyFreelance Visa (Freiberufler)Up to 3 yrSufficient income / contractsBerlin / Munich hubs; freelance only, not employee
CroatiaDigital Nomad ResidenceUp to 1 yr~€2,540 / moTax-exempt on foreign income for nomad visa-holders
GreeceDigital Nomad Visa1 yr renewable to 3~€3,500 / mo50% income-tax reduction first 7 yr
ItalyDigital Nomad & Remote Worker Visa1 yr renewable~€28K / yrLaunched 2024; highly-skilled requirement
MexicoTemporary Resident VisaUp to 4 yr~$2,600 USD / moPopular CDMX / Oaxaca / Mérida hubs
Costa RicaRentista / Digital Nomad Visa1 yr renewable~$3,000 USD / moStrong Internet infrastructure; tax-favorable
Barbados12-Month Welcome Stamp12 mo (renewable)~$50K USD / yrFirst Caribbean nomad visa; English-speaking
UAE (Dubai)Virtual Working Programme1 yr renewable~$3,500 USD / moNo income tax in UAE
JapanDigital Nomad Visa6 mo (non-renewable)~¥10M (~$67K USD) / yrLaunched April 2024; tech-friendly

Remote-Only Job Boards & Recruiting Channels

Curated channel inventory used by Career Services to surface remote-friendly roles to commuter students. Each board has different rigor on the “remote” claim — column 3 indicates whether listings are remote-by-default or simply remote-eligible.

ChannelFocusRemote rigorNotes for students
We Work RemotelyCross-functional, eng-heavyRemote-by-defaultHigh-quality; oldest dedicated remote board
Remote.coCross-functionalRemote-by-defaultCurated; strong for early-career
FlexJobsVetted remote + flexRemote-by-defaultPaid membership; high signal/noise
Working NomadsGlobal remoteRemote-by-defaultAggregator; surfaces non-US-only roles
RemotiveTech + productRemote-by-defaultCurated; quarterly remote salary report
JustRemoteCross-functionalRemote-by-defaultRegion-filtered (US-only / EU-only)
HimalayasGlobal remote techRemote-by-defaultStrong company-culture data per listing
Dynamite JobsOperations, marketingRemote-by-defaultFounder Tropical MBA community
AngelList Talent / WellfoundStartup rolesRemote-eligible filterDirect founder messaging; early-stage
LinkedIn (Remote filter)All sectorsRemote-eligible filterLargest pool; some listings hybrid-only
Indeed (Remote filter)All sectorsRemote-eligible filterLargest US pool; mixed quality
Built In (Remote filter)TechRemote-eligible filterChicago-anchored network; useful for Illinois Tech students
ToptalVetted contractor talentRemote-by-defaultSelective; high day-rates
AndelaVetted technical talentRemote-by-defaultGlobal; engineering / data / design
CrossoverFull-time remote rolesRemote-by-defaultMid-level + senior; rigorous testing
Hubstaff Talent / Arc.devTech contractorsRemote-by-defaultFree; rates skew lower; useful entry pool

Proposed New Remote-Work Badges (4)

Four additions to the proposed Crosswalk slate that specifically credential the remote-work skill set. Aligned to NACE Teamwork & Technology, WEF 2025 Distributed Work cluster, DOE ESF Workplace Skills, and the Burning Glass Hybrid Job Economy taxonomy.

1. Remote Work Readiness Practitioner

Completion of the 6-module workshop plus a portfolio artifact (home-office setup write-up, async-collab case study, remote-job application package).

Y2 (~25–35 hr) · Tier L2

2. Distributed Collaboration Specialist

Documented async-first collaboration on a real project — written RFCs, time-zone-aware planning, documented decisions, peer assessment from distributed teammates.

Y2–Y3 (~40–80 hr) · Tier L3

3. Digital Nomad Strategist

Country-specific feasibility study (visa, tax, cost-of-living, healthcare, time-zone overlap) for a chosen distributed-work destination. Reviewed by Career Services + an alumni mentor with relevant experience.

Y3–Y4 (~15–25 hr) · Tier L3

4. Remote Job Search Strategist

Curated remote-job application pipeline: tailored resume + Loom intro + 10 targeted applications via remote-by-default boards + 3 informational interviews with remote-first hiring managers.

Y2–Y4 (~20–40 hr) · Tier L3

Pilot Sequencing & Success Metrics

PhaseTermActionSuccess metric
Pilot 1Fall 2026Launch Workshop Core (6 modules) as Y2 elective25 students complete; 80% report likelihood of pursuing a remote-first role
Pilot 2Spring 2027Issue Remote Work Readiness Practitioner badge to first cohort; integrate with Career Services 1:1 coaching15 badges issued; first 5 students apply to remote-first roles via job-board curriculum
ScaleFall 2027Add Distributed Collaboration Specialist; integrate with IPRO async-team projects50 students complete workshop; 30 earn at least one remote-work badge
GlobalizeSpring 2028Launch Digital Nomad Strategist + Remote Job Search Strategist; partner with international alumni network10 students complete Digital Nomad Strategist with reviewed feasibility studies
OutcomeFall 2028First-cohort longitudinal study: 6-month employment outcomes vs non-track peersRemote-track students at >= peer-cohort 6-month employment rate; >30% in remote-first roles

Fred maintaining a curated list of gaming-adjacent hackathons with $30K–$100K prize tiers. Targeting Spring 2027 cohort entries with Elevate badge attribution for placement and prize wins.

The 3 Proposed Elevate × Esports Pilot Activities

Drafted May 2026; awaiting April’s sign-off for immediate implementation. Each activity passes the curriculum-based-and-skill-focused test, requires a tangible artifact, and awards Elevate badges that translate to LinkedIn-displayable credentials.

Checklist Item #1 · Gaming Content Creator Business Development

Students develop a content-creator business plan and execute a pilot project, learning the skills required to build a sustainable presence as an independent gaming content creator. Directly addresses the Esports Center’s strategic priority of teaching students how to earn as independent creators.

Year eligibilityYear 1 or Year 2 (elective)
Time commitment15–25 hours across semester
VerificationPortfolio submission + 400-word business reflection
Badges awardedInnovation Catalyst · Digital Literacy Champion (L3) · Leadership Development · Professional Communications
LocationEsports and Digital Arts Center, MTCC — or virtual

Requirements: Attend Esports Center orientation · Complete business canvas (niche, audience, monetization) · Establish monetized presence on Twitch / YouTube / TikTok / Discord · Publish 3–5 pieces of approved content OR 3 hours of streaming · Submit portfolio + reflection.

Why it matters: Creator economy projected $28.6B → $230.4B by 2034 (Market.us). Builds transferable content strategy, audience analytics, brand management, and digital marketing skills.

Checklist Item #2 · Esports Event Production & Management

Students gain hands-on experience in esports event production by helping plan, promote, and execute a gaming event or tournament. Mirrors professional event-management roles found at leading collegiate esports programs.

Year eligibilityYear 1 or Year 2 (elective)
Time commitment12–20 hours across semester
VerificationEvent documentation + supervisor confirmation + 400-word reflection
Badges awardedCollaboration Champion · Professional Communications · Leadership Development (L2–L3) · Career Critical Thinker
LocationEsports and Digital Arts Center, MTCC

Requirements: Attend planning meetings with Esports leadership · Take ownership of ONE component (marketing, registration, stream production, logistics, sponsorship) · Participate in event execution · Submit documentation + reflection.

Why it matters: Esports = $4.8B global industry with 640M+ viewers; projected to exceed $25B by 2035 (Statista; Future Market Insights). Builds transferable event-planning, project-management, marketing, and logistics skills.

Checklist Item #3 · Game Design & Development Foundations

Students explore gaming industry fundamentals by completing a gaming-related course or certification and connecting classroom learning to career development through a brief practical reflection.

Year eligibilityYear 1 or Year 2 (elective)
Time commitmentCourse duration + 1–2 hours reflection
VerificationCourse transcript / certificate / digital badge + 250-word reflection
Badges awardedDigital Literacy Champion (L2) · Career Critical Thinker
Approved optionsIllinois Tech courses (Game Design & Experiential Media, CS with gaming applications, Digital Media & Interactive Design) · External certifications (Unity Certified User, Unreal Engine fundamentals, Coursera / edX game design courses) · Esports Center workshops

Why it matters: Bridges curricular learning with co-curricular career development. Gaming industry projected to exceed $300B globally by 2027 (Newzoo). Builds technical and creative skills valued across tech, entertainment, and media industries.

Emerging Track A — Esports × Data Analytics

Source & reference. April shared a case study showing how Cloud9 and other top esports teams use Microsoft Azure, Power BI, and a structured data-science workflow to gain competitive advantages. This frames a natural extension of the Elevate Data Analytics Professional badge into the esports vertical.

Proposed pilot activity (draft). Students select a competitive title (e.g., Valorant, League of Legends, Rocket League), pull match telemetry / replay data, build a Power BI or Tableau dashboard surfacing player-performance and team-strategy signals, and present findings to the Esports Center coaching staff. Verification = dashboard artifact + 500-word analytical reflection + coach Q&A.

Badge alignment. Data Analytics Professional (primary) · AI & ML Explorer (secondary) · Critical Thinker L3 · Pro Comms L3 · the proposed Quantitative Reasoning & Data Storytelling Expert badge.

Emerging Track B — Biometrics × Esports

Source & reference. April shared a Novus Attractions article on biometric player analytics — eye tracking, heart-rate variability, reaction time, posture, and motion tracking — revolutionizing personalized gaming experiences. The career-pathway implications span health tech, UX research, sensor engineering, and performance optimization.

Proposed pilot activity (draft). Cross-school project linking pre-health, applied psychology, computer science, and game design students. Deliverable: a small-N biometric study (e.g., 5–10 willing volunteers from the Esports Discord, IRB review where applicable) measuring one performance variable, with a written interpretation grounded in published sport-science literature. Verification = study design memo + data summary + 500-word reflection.

Badge alignment. Research Contributor (primary) · AI & ML Explorer (secondary, where ML-on-biosignals) · the proposed Pre-Health Clinical Readiness Specialist badge for Pre-Health-track contributors · Cross-Domain Specialist for multi-school teams.

Exelon Summer Institute — Summer 2026 Camps & Activations

April Welch directs the Exelon Summer Institute alongside the Esports and Digital Arts Center. The Summer 2026 camps and activations are being reimagined with Dan and Anthony, with explicit room for an Elevate-aligned credentialing layer.

Elevate’s contribution. Pre-college pathway integration — high-school participants who complete summer institute modules become eligible for the Pre-College Pioneer badge and receive a head-start on Year 1 Elevate activities upon Illinois Tech enrollment. Camp curriculum modules (esports operations, data analytics, biometrics, content creation) map directly to the three pilot Elevate activities above, creating an end-to-end pipeline from summer outreach to four-year career-readiness credential.

Gaming-Adjacent Hackathons — $30K–$100K Prize Tier (curated Spring 2027 target list)

Curated list maintained by Career Services for cohort entry. Wins and placements qualify for the proposed Hackathon & Competitive Programming Achiever milestone badge.

Categories represented: game-jam competitions (Global Game Jam, Brackeys, GMTK), serious-games / edu-games challenges, gaming-AI hackathons (NVIDIA, Unity AI), biometric / health-game hackathons, esports-analytics datathons, and Web3 / blockchain-gaming sponsored events. Full list available from Career Services on request.

Why This Partnership Wins — Structural Advantages

Co-located in MTCC

Esports & Digital Arts Center and Career Services share McCormick Tribune Campus Center. Walk-in collaboration; no scheduling overhead.

1,211+ Discord community

Illinois Tech Esports Discord = built-in recruitment pool for any pilot activity announcement. Higher conversion than email-only outreach.

Strategic-Initiatives backing

April Welch’s role as AVP Strategic Initiatives gives the partnership institutional weight and a direct line to senior leadership when scaling.

Pre-college pipeline

Exelon Summer Institute generates a steady inbound pipeline of admitted students already exposed to the gaming credentialing track.

Game Design & Experiential Media degree

Illinois Tech’s degree program supplies a curricular partner for the Game Design & Development Foundations activity. Reduces design / build cost for Career Services.

Fundraiser donor narrative

Elevate badge artifacts give the Esports computer-revamp fundraiser tangible donor-facing evidence of student outcomes — not just access, but credentialed access.

Hackathons & Competitions

Build-week sprints, innovation days, and business case competitions across schools

IPRO
Innovation Day (Y2 required)
Multiple
Annual Hackathons
Case
Business Case Comp (Stuart)

IPRO Innovation Day is the flagship cross-disciplinary competition; required Year 2 activity. Earns Leadership Development L2–L3 + Innovation Pioneer badge.

Hackathons: Multiple across schools (Computing, Armour, Kaplan Startup Studio). Job simulations and hackathons are an approved Elevator Pitch Wild Card. Badges: Innovation Pioneer, Teamwork (Leadership L1–L3), Career Critical Thinker.

Business Case Competition (Stuart Tier 3, 20–30 hours): internal or external case competition applying analytical and strategic thinking. Badges: Critical Thinking + Communication.

Expanded Cross-Curricular Competition Catalog

The eight canonical events above cover the institutional core. The slate below extends Elevate’s competition portfolio across every school and every framework pillar — engineering, pre-health, architecture, design, math/data, policy, sustainability, communication, and the humanities. Every entry maps to at least one foundational track and one milestone-eligible outcome, and each can be submitted for badge attribution through either the canonical activity catalog or the Wild Card pathway.

Schools and partner units can propose new competitions for canonical-catalog inclusion by submitting a competition brief to Career Services & Experiential Learning. Review cycle: rolling, with quarterly publication updates.

Strategic Rollup — Why This Matters for Elevate

Coverage

8 canonical events plus 41 sector-specific competitions across 8 sectors. Every Illinois Tech school has at least four named competitions; every Elevate foundational track has at least six competition pathways.

Workload Distribution

Hour estimates span 5–15 hrs (Forage simulations) through 100–200 hrs (EPA P3, SAE series) — giving Elevate Success Coaches calibrated options for every student capacity.

Badge Linkage

Every entry credentials at least one Elevate foundational tier and one milestone-eligible outcome. Several entries also seed the 12 proposed new badges identified in the Crosswalk page.

Verification Standard

Standard portfolio-based verification — team roster, deliverable artifact, reflection — with placement / prize wins eligible for the proposed Hackathon & Competitive Programming Achiever milestone badge.

Wild-Card Submission Pathway

If a competition or hackathon is not on the canonical list, students may submit it through the Elevator Pitch Wild Card (Y1) or Flight Plan Wild Card (Y2). The submission must include: event name, sponsor, hours invested, deliverables produced, and a 400-word reflection. Career Services reviews and approves wild cards based on rigor and validity.

Approved as wild cards in past cycles: external industry hackathons (Devpost, MLH events), university case competitions (Notre Dame, Northwestern), and personal startup projects with documented mentorship.

28 Specialized Badges — Partnership Tracks

Specialized badges live at the intersection of Elevate’s framework architecture and a specific partner ecosystem (school, vendor, or pilot population). Every specialized badge maps back to one or more foundational tracks plus at least one milestone category, so a student earning a specialized credential simultaneously progresses on their foundational and milestone portfolio.

Pre-Health Pathway (5 — Proposed)

BadgeTarget Pathway
Future MDMedical school pathway: MCAT prep + clinical hours + service learning + research project.
Future DOOsteopathic medical pathway with osteopathic principles shadowing + clinical hours.
Future PAPhysician Assistant pathway: clinical hours + GRE prep + healthcare experience.
Future PharmacistPharmD pathway: pharmacy technician experience + PCAT prep + community service.

Esports & Digital Arts Pathway (4 — Emerging)

BadgeEarned When
Game Design FoundationsComplete 10–15 hr game-design project; portfolio artifact required.
Esports Event Production Specialist12–20 hr tournament-operations role; broadcasting or production responsibilities.
Gaming Content Creator15–25 hr streamer / content-creator project + monetization or sponsorship documentation.
Esports Analytics Researcher15–25 hr analytics project with documented data analysis; connects to Digital Lit L3.

How Specialized Badges Stack with Foundational + Milestone

Every specialized badge cross-walks to one or more foundational tracks plus at least one milestone category. A student earning Future CFO simultaneously progresses on Financial Literacy + Career Critical Thinker (foundational), Certification Achiever (milestone), and the Stuart C-Suite specialized pathway — three forms of evidence from one credential. This stacking is the design principle behind the 73-badge architecture and the reason Elevate’s credentials carry signal weight that single-credential systems do not.

Resource Needs & Requests

Critical Needs

Additional full-time staff: Current 1-person model managing 1,152 students, 5 coaches (academic-year cumulative), 6+ platforms, 8+ partnerships, and 53-page dashboard is unsustainable for scale. Recommend 1 additional FTE for program coordination.

Dedicated Elevate budget line: Currently pulling from general Career Services budget. A dedicated Elevate budget would enable better planning and accountability.

Student staff continuity: Sai and Yogesh are graduating (~19–20 hours/week opening), and approximately 12 hours of weekly allotment remain unused. Spring / Fall 2026 hiring round should backfill both gaps to preserve Year 1 & Year 2 coaching coverage and platform-support capacity.

Growth Opportunities

Grant funding: Elevate’s framework alignment (8 national standards) positions it well for DOL, NSF, and foundation grants for career readiness innovation.

Employer sponsorship: Badge verification and talent-pipeline access could generate employer partnership revenue.

Scaling model: Elevate’s badge architecture and dashboard framework could be licensed or adapted by peer institutions, creating potential revenue and recognition.

Return on Investment Indicators

398 students enrolled with 1 FTE manager — cost per student served is among the lowest for structured career-readiness programs nationally.

AWS Academy: $0 curriculum cost providing $1,000+ value per student in certification exam discounts and training materials.

Employment guarantee: Direct enrollment and retention value. Students choosing Illinois Tech partially based on the Elevate promise represent tuition revenue preservation.

Dashboard: Developed internally at $0 external cost, providing reporting capability that would cost $50K–$100K+ through external vendors.

Full-Time Staff Contribution
$200K+
FTE-equivalent annual value delivered

The Experiential Learning Manager’s 50–60 hour weekly investment across program design, partnership development, platform management, coach supervision, analytics, and this 40-page dashboard represents an estimated $200K+ equivalent in combined strategy, technology, and project-management value delivered by a single FTE.

Glossary

Plain-English definitions of every Elevate term, acronym, platform name, and partner organization referenced across the dashboard.

Program Terms

TermDefinition
ElevateIllinois Tech’s institutional career-readiness initiative. A four-year (Y1–Y4, with Y5 if needed) co-curricular framework that maps every student’s experiential learning journey to verified, evidence-based skill development from pre-college through graduation, anchored by the Elevate Guarantee.
Elevate GuaranteeInstitutional commitment: if a student completes all Elevate requirements and an employment or graduate-school offer is not achieved within six months of graduation, the student may be eligible to enroll tuition-free in select graduate-level courses at Illinois Tech (up to 9 credit hours) or participate cost-free in relevant up-skilling programs or digital apprenticeships, within one year of their graduation date.
Elevate ExperienceA required experiential activity (e.g., career fair, networking event, internship, competition, research) that counts toward program completion. Each Y1–Y4 year has minimum-expected-requirements plus two electives.
Elevate My Future PlanA personalized co-curricular and career pathway developed with advisers, Elevate Success Coaches, and peer mentors during Y1. Mapped on the Elevate platform; reviewed each term.
Co-Curricular PlanStudent-developed plan documenting intended Elevate activities for the upcoming term, reviewed during Elevate Success Coach 1:1s. 122 plans filed as of May 2026 (97 Y1 + 25 Y2).
Co-Curricular TranscriptOfficial Illinois Tech record of verified Elevate activities and badge earnings, generated through 312.
Program ReachPercentage of eligible students (first-year admitted) who are actively enrolled in Elevate — 34.4% engagement rate (137 unique earners / 398 enrolled) as of May 2026.
Completion RatePercentage of enrolled students who have completed all required activities for their current program year. Three-bucket status model (May 12 2026 tracker): 332 IN PROGRESS (237 Y1 + 95 Y2 — signed T&C, activities still pending) · 42 CLOSE TO FINISHED (33 Y1 + 9 Y2 — actively progressing through required activities) · 24 COMPLETE (20 Y1 + 4 Y2 — finished all required activities). All three buckets represent active program participation. Net movement since May 10: –22 In Progress, +20 Close to Finished, +2 Complete.
Wild CardStudent-proposed custom experience submitted for review and badge attribution. Two slots per year — Elevator Pitch Wild Card (Y1) and Flight Plan Wild Card (Y2). Submission requires event name, sponsor, hours, deliverables, and a 400-word reflection.
First Destination SurveyAnnual graduating-class outcomes survey via Handshake; the data source for Career Services outcomes reporting.
LAMP MethodJob application strategy framework taught in Year 4: Lead, Attractive, Mass, Personalized.
T&CElevate Terms and Conditions. The institutional agreement students sign on 312 to formally enroll in Elevate. 398 students have signed as of May 2026.

Roles & Coaching

TermDefinition
Elevate Success CoachStudent staff role providing 1:1 coaching and progress monitoring for assigned Y1 (and select Y2) students. 95 sessions / month with 91.6% first-time meetings as of March 2026.
Cohort ManagerStudent staff role supporting a year-specific cohort with administrative coordination, platform support, and outreach.
Experiential Learning ManagerCareer Services role responsible for Elevate architecture, partnerships, and dashboard development. Currently Dr. Freddrick Logan, PhD.
ELM (Empowerment, Leadership & Mentorship)Peer mentorship training pathway. ELM Practitioner (L1, 3–5 hr foundational), ELM-Specialist (L2), ELM Certified Peer Mentor (L3, 15+ hr full training).

Badge System Terms

TermDefinition
Digital BadgeA verified digital credential issued through Campus Groups (312) representing demonstrated competency in a specific skill area or achievement. Shareable to LinkedIn via native integration.
Foundational BadgeCore skill track badge across 5 tracks (Critical Thinking, Digital Literacy, Leadership, Communication, Collaboration) with 5 tier levels each — 25 total in the canonical architecture.
Milestone BadgeSingle-level achievement badge organized into 4 color-coded categories (Program Progression, Career Preparedness, Character Development, Special Achievements) — 20 total.
Specialized BadgePartnership-specific badge (Stuart C-Suite, AWS Academy 17, Transfer 8, Pre-Health, ELM Mentorship, Esports) — 28 total. Lives at intersection of foundational + milestone.
Tier Level (L1–L5)Progressive skill levels: L1 Foundation (1–5 hr) · L2 Skill Builder (5–15 hr) · L3 Proficient (15–40 hr) · L4 Advanced (40–100 hr) · L5 Elevator (100+ hr, system transformation).
Elevator (L5)Highest tier in the badge progression system. Represents system transformation and creating new solutions; 100+ hours typical.
C-Suite PathwayStuart School partnership badge series — Future CEO / Future COO (auto-earn: CAPM) / Future CFO (auto-earn: SIE 70%+) / Future CTO / Future CMO — requiring specific activity + skill combinations.
Auto-EarnBadge automatically awarded upon completion of a qualifying certification or trigger event (e.g., CAPM → Future COO; SIE 70%+ → Future CFO).
CrosswalkMatrix mapping the 73 Elevate badges to the 8 foundational frameworks (NACE, P21, WEF 2030, AAC&U HIPs, O*NET, CASEL, DOE ESF, Kolb ELT) with primary / secondary / supporting alignment coding.
Verification MethodHow a badge is awarded. Four methods: Automatic (312 tracking) · Portfolio (student evidence + reflection) · External (supervisor / cert) · Peer (presentation / review).
Stacked CredentialA single accomplishment that simultaneously progresses foundational + milestone + specialized badges — the core design principle of the 73-badge architecture.

Platforms & Tools

TermDefinition
312 (CampusGroups)CampusGroups platform, branded internally as 312 (after Chicago’s area code). Institution-wide subscription. Hosts checklists, badges, events, communities, co-curricular transcript. 312.iit.edu
Elevate PortalPublic-facing virtual career portal, powered by the uConnect platform. elevate.iit.edu
uConnectThe underlying technology platform powering elevate.iit.edu — the public-facing virtual career center.
HandshakeNational platform for student profiles, employer engagement, job applications, and First Destination Survey data.
Simplicity / CSMCareer-services case management platform; internship form processing and student outreach.
CredlyExternal-facing digital credential platform with public verification URLs and downloadable certificates. Currently in use by CLI (over seat limits / cost).
TalonLong-range talent / credential platform on the Elevate roadmap. Kaplan-adjacent. Backend target for the Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform vision.
CourseraCoursera Career Academy partnership; 200K+ non-credit learners on Illinois Tech’s Coursera platform. Anchors the lifelong-learning / non-credit pillar.
Ed2GoCLI non-credit professional courses launched with revenue share. Summer 2026 target: integrate with Elevate optional / elective tracks for stackable badge + completion.
VMock / Quinncia / ForageCareer-tool stack — AI resume scoring (VMock), mock interviews (Quinncia), virtual internships (Forage). Used across Y1–Y4 activity flow.
CanvasIllinois Tech LMS — course-credit pathways (BRVN 200, Stuart Career Edge).
DCC (MIT)MIT Digital Credentials Consortium. Open-source infrastructure for verifiable, portable, learner-owned digital credentials. Future-state credentialing layer for 312 + Credly + Talon. digitalcredentials.mit.edu

Acronyms

AcronymFull Name / Definition
NACENational Association of Colleges and Employers — 8 career-readiness competencies updated annually.
P21Partnership for 21st Century Learning — the 4 Cs framework (Critical Thinking, Communication, Collaboration, Creativity) plus literacies.
WEFWorld Economic Forum — Future of Jobs 2025 report.
CASELCollaborative for Academic, Social, and Emotional Learning — 5 SEL competencies.
AAC&UAssociation of American Colleges & Universities — 11 High-Impact Practices.
HIPsHigh-Impact Practices — AAC&U-validated educational practices.
O*NETU.S. Department of Labor Occupational Information Network — 12 core occupational skills.
DOE ESFU.S. Department of Education Employability Skills Framework — 3 categories × 11 sub-skills.
ELTKolb’s Experiential Learning Theory — the design backbone of the Elevate tier system.
FYEFirst-Year Experience — the year-one onboarding and exposure module.
ELMEmpowerment, Leadership, and Mentorship — peer-mentor training pathway.
CAPMCertified Associate in Project Management (PMI) — Future COO auto-earn trigger.
SIESecurities Industry Essentials — Future CFO auto-earn trigger (70%+ pass).
AMAAmerican Marketing Association — Future CMO auto-earn trigger (chapter membership).
AWSAmazon Web Services — AWS Academy partnership covering 17 certification pathways.
CPSChicago Public Schools.
IPROInterprofessional Projects — required Y2 cross-disciplinary innovation activity.
DAU / WAU / MAUDaily / Weekly / Monthly Active Users — platform engagement measurement standard.
CMCCareer Management Center (Stuart School of Business).
CLICenter for Learning Innovation — recently absorbed OPCE; future home for university-wide credentialing.
OPCEOffice of Professional and Continuing Education — absorbed into CLI.
SGAStudent Government Association — emerging Elevate partnership for civic-engagement and leadership credentialing.
DCCMIT Digital Credentials Consortium — vendor-neutral verifiable-credentials infrastructure.
FERPA / HIPAAFamily Educational Rights and Privacy Act / Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — the two federal privacy frameworks Elevate complies with.
MTCCMcCormick Tribune Campus Center — home of Career Services Suite 106 and the Esports & Digital Arts Center.

Elevate Terms & Conditions (T&C) Summary

The Elevate Terms & Conditions are presented to students upon enrollment. Students must digitally sign on 312 before accessing program activities and earning badges. As of May 2026, 398 students have signed (290 Y1 + 108 Y2). The full T&C document is on the Terms & Conditions page; key commitments summarized here:

  • Program commitment. Multi-year career-readiness program; complete required activities each year.
  • Employment Guarantee eligibility. Complete all required activities; maintain good academic + conduct standing; active engagement with Career Services.
  • Required activities. Elevate Success Coach check-ins, resume development, mentorship engagement, student-organization participation, professional experience hours, community engagement.
  • Badge system participation. Tracked through 312; aligned to NACE, P21, WEF, CASEL, AAC&U HIPs, O*NET, DOE ESF, Kolb ELT frameworks.
  • Data & privacy. FERPA-compliant; aggregated, de-identified data for institutional reporting only.
  • Code of conduct. Professional representation across mentorship, employer, community, and online interactions.
  • Communication. Active Illinois Tech email; timely responses to program outreach.
  • Platform usage. 312 Portal, Elevate Portal, Handshake, CampusGroups profiles maintained per program-year direction.

Collaborator contact information: for full names and contact details of any collaborators referenced across the dashboard, please contact Dr. Freddrick Logan (flogan1@illinoistech.edu).

Handshake & Campus Groups Timeline (placeholder — pending Outlook scan)

This timeline will auto-populate from Outlook once authorization completes. Expected structure:

  • 2025-Q3: Initial API discovery conversations with Handshake and Campus Groups; account-rep contacts identified.
  • 2025-Q4: Sandbox credential requests; FERPA review by OTS (Patrick); admin-access incident resolution on Campus Groups.
  • 2026-Q1: Multi-tab dashboard launched with month-toggle; manual data flow from 312 to dashboard via CSV exports.
  • 2026-Q2: Talon scoping; India dev team conversations; Handshake First Destination Survey alignment.

  • Successes: Sub-1-day Career Services platform onboarding for the Stuart cohort, BRVN 200 launch coordination, AWS Academy 17-pathway integration, Braven MOU signed.

Technical Requirements — Software Product

Build specification for the Elevate Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform Product

Working spec for the platform we are building. Audience: India development team, Talon engineering, Illinois Tech ITS, and external partners. Updated continuously as scoping conversations progress.

Architecture pillars

1. Identity & Access

SSO via Illinois Tech IdP (Microsoft 365 / Azure AD). Student / staff / employer roles. FERPA-aligned permissions matrix.

2. Badge Issuance Engine

Open Badges 3.0 / Verifiable Credentials. Automated triggers for stacked badges (Future CEO/COO/CFO/CMO/CTO). Audit log.

3. Evidence Store

Encrypted storage of student artifacts (resumes, reflections, portfolio links). FERPA-compliant retention policy.

4. API Gateway

REST + GraphQL endpoints for partner systems. OAuth 2.0. Rate limiting. Versioning.

5. Event Bus

Pub/sub for badge-earned, checklist-completed, T&C-signed events. Webhooks for downstream notifications.

6. Analytics Layer

Real-time KPIs feeding this dashboard. Cohort analytics, completion funnels, badge-distribution dashboards.

7. AI / ML Services

Personalized badge recommendations, Chiron AI avatars, Talent platform matching, framework alignment suggestions.

8. Forms & Assessments

Year-specific forms (Pre-college, Y1, Y2, Y3, Y4 pilot, Transfer Bridge). NACE 2026 + Coursera 2026 self-assessment items.

Non-Functional Requirements

  • FERPA compliance: No raw PII in public surfaces. Aggregated counts default; role-gated views for staff.
  • Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA. Keyboard navigation. Screen-reader friendly.
  • Performance: <1.5s first contentful paint. <200ms median API response.
  • Reliability: 99.9% uptime SLA. Daily backups. Disaster-recovery RTO <4h.
  • Security: SOC 2 Type II readiness; encryption at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3); regular penetration tests.
  • Internationalization: Multi-language UI (priority: English, Spanish, Mandarin) for global learner population.
  • Mobile: Mobile-first responsive design; Year 2 population is 99% commuter.
  • Audit trail: Every badge issuance and form submission has timestamp, actor, and verification source.

Functional Requirements — By Persona

PersonaMust-Do (P0)Should-Do (P1)Could-Do (P2)
StudentSign T&C; view checklist; submit reflections; view earned badges; track Co-Curricular PlanMobile drafting (localStorage); Loom-based artifact upload; LinkedIn share; portfolio linkAI-recommended next activity; peer leaderboard (opt-in)
Elevate Success CoachView caseload; log check-ins; flag at-risk; verify portfolio submissionsBulk-message cohort; intervention typology log; reschedule auto-suggestAI summary of student progress; ML at-risk scoring
Career Services StaffIssue badges manually; configure forms; export FERPA-safe aggregate reportsWorkflow-builder for new activities; bulk badge issuance; T&C version controlMulti-template form-builder; localized form rendering
Faculty PartnerView school-specific cohort; assign activity credit; co-sign partnership badgesSurface faculty-mentored research opportunities to the Elevate newsletterFaculty Innovation Fund management UI
EmployerFilter candidates by earned badges; export FERPA-safe candidate list; post jobs via Handshake bridgeSponsor a cohort; co-brand Faculty Innovation Fund experience; host virtual internshipsAI candidate matching against role rubric; Talent platform integration
Senior LeadershipRead executive dashboard; monthly KPI snapshot; budget & resource snapshotCustom audience cuts; export print-ready PDF; benchmark vs. peer institutionsPredictive enrollment + outcomes modeling

Target Tech Stack (Working Draft)

LayerCandidateRationale
FrontendReact + TypeScript + Tailwind; static export (Next.js / Vite); print-readyModern accessibility tooling; sustainable single-developer build today
Backend APITalon (long-term) / Node + GraphQL bridge (interim)Mallik-directed long-term backend; bridge solution sustains current dashboard until Talon ships
Identity / SSOMicrosoft 365 / Azure AD (Illinois Tech IdP)Already in place institution-wide; no extra vendor
Badge issuance312 (CampusGroups) internal; Credly external (hybrid); DCC-alignedPer the CLI hybrid strategy — preserves automation + external verifiability
Data warehouseSnowflake / BigQuery (TBD); read replicas for analyticsStandard higher-ed pattern; supports growth without rebuild
Event busKafka / SQS (managed)Async badge / form events; downstream webhook delivery
AI / MLAzure OpenAI (existing Illinois Tech contract); Anthropic Claude for high-context tasksPer Mallik direction; institutional contract reduces procurement friction
HostingGitHub Pages (current dashboard) → AWS / Azure cloud (production)Lowest-cost interim; cloud-native target for scale + ITS integration
ObservabilityDatadog / Sentry; structured loggingStandard SaaS pattern; usage stays within ITS-approved vendors

SystemDirectionContractOwner

HandshakeBidirectionalPull profiles, jobs, First Destination Survey; push Elevate badge metadataCareer Services

Delivery Milestones

MilestoneTarget WindowOwnerExit Criteria
M1 · Architecture reviewSummer 2026Fred + India dev team + Talon engineering8-pillar architecture signed off; integration contracts approved
M2 · API gateway + IdentityFall 2026India dev teamSSO live; 312 + Handshake pulls authenticated; OAuth flow tested
M3 · Badge issuance engineFall 2026 – Spring 2027Career Services + India dev teamOpen Badges 3.0 issued; stacked-badge triggers automated
M4 · Employer Badge DashboardSpring 2027Career Services + Employer PartnershipsExternal verifier link working; first 5 employers piloted
M5 · DCC alignment pilotFall 2027CLI + Career ServicesFirst Elevate badge issued in DCC-verifiable format with public verifier URL
M6 · AI services liveAY 2027–28India dev team + Office of Digital LearningPersonalized recommendations + Chiron AI avatars in production

Out-of-Scope Guardrails

  • Clinical / diagnostic instruments. Pearson’s clinical, psychological, neuropsychological, behavioral-health, and any mental-health screening / diagnostic catalogs are explicitly out of scope. They belong to qualified clinicians under Student Health and Wellness with separate FERPA + HIPAA governance.
  • Public student PII. No raw names / emails / IDs in dashboard surfaces. First-name-only and role-gated views require authenticated staff context outside the static site.
  • External advertising / monetization. No third-party ads, no learner data resale, no marketing-platform data sharing.
  • Replacement of academic Registrar functions. Co-curricular transcript supplements the academic transcript — it does not replace, modify, or override Registrar systems.

Elevate Program Book — Multimedia E-Booklets

Three audience-specific e-booklets in production: Students, Alumni, Corporate Partners

Multimedia project. Each e-booklet is a self-contained digital publication combining text, interactive elements, audio interviews, video stories, framework overlays, and shareable badge-portfolio links. Each audience version pulls from the same content base but adapts voice, depth, and call-to-action.

1 · Students E-Booklet

Y1–Y4
Cohort Coverage
6 mo
Employment Guarantee
73
Badges to Earn
80+
Activities Available

Sections

  • Welcome to Elevate — The Guarantee, the 1890 founding story, what your four years look like.
  • Your Pathway by Year — Pre-college, Y1 Exposure, Y2 Engagement, Y3 Accountability, Y4 Upskilling (pilot).
  • The Badge System — 5 foundational tracks, 5 tier levels, milestone badges, specialized pathways (Stuart C-Suite, AWS, Esports).
  • Your Coach & Co-Curricular Plan — 1:1 cadence, SMART goals, evidence rules.
  • How to Choose Optional Activities — 60+ electives, decision aids, framework anchors.
  • Impact & Pathway Scenarios — Actual May 2026 engagement signals (verifiable) plus intended pathway-design scenarios (composite personas A./R./J./P. illustrating how badge progression is intended to compound, not real student outcomes yet).
  • Multimedia: 30-second cohort welcome video, audio testimonials, interactive badge tree, “What’s next” planner.

Intellectual Property & Attribution

Documentation of authorship, intellectual property rights, and usage restrictions for the Elevate initiative, its frameworks, and this dashboard.

Notice. The intellectual property documented on this page has been formally recorded with Illinois Institute of Technology. Mimicking, reproducing, or repeating this work without proper attribution is strictly prohibited and documented with the institution.

Primary Author & Architect

Dr. Freddrick Logan, PhD

Title: Experiential Learning Manager, Career Services & Experiential Learning
Institution: Illinois Institute of Technology
Contact: flogan1@illinoistech.edu
Role: Sole designer, developer, and architect of the Elevate initiative — including all program frameworks, badge systems, partnership models, operational processes, and this executive dashboard.

The majority of the conceptual design, strategic framework, implementation planning, technical development, data analysis, and partnership cultivation documented in this dashboard was conceived and executed by Dr. Freddrick Logan.

Scope of Intellectual Property

The following elements constitute original intellectual property developed by Dr. Freddrick Logan for Illinois Institute of Technology:

#IP ElementDescription
1Elevate Framework ArchitectureMulti-year structured career-readiness pathway with escalating Year 1–Year 5 requirements, badge progression model, and the Elevate Guarantee employment-or-tuition-free structure.
2Badge System Design (73 canonical)5-level foundational badge hierarchy (L1–L5), 20 milestone badges across 4 color categories, 28 specialized partnership badges, and the 8-framework alignment matrix (NACE · P21 · WEF 2030 · AAC&U HIPs · O*NET · CASEL · DOE ESF · Kolb ELT).
3Elevate Success Coach Operations ModelTraining program, supervision structure, meeting frameworks, intervention typology, caseload management, 91.6% first-time-meeting rate operating playbook.
4Partnership Integration ModelsStructured approach to integrating academic-school partnerships (Stuart 18 activities + C-Suite; Armour AWS Academy scaling; Architecture; Pre-Health; Kaplan Institute) with Career Services badge tracking. Also: Braven inside the catalog; Athletics, Esports, CLI, SGA cross-cut partnerships.
5Specialized Track DesignsCommuter Student + Remote & Hybrid Work Readiness expansion; Transfer Cohort Bridge Pathway (9 transfer badges); Esports & Digital Arts; Quantum Computing; Athletics; Pre-Health career pathways with associated activities, hours, evidence requirements, and badge mappings.
6Co-Curricular Plan ModelStructured planning process, form architecture, artifact documentation system on the 312 (CampusGroups) platform — 122 plans filed (97 Y1 + 25 Y2 as of May 2026).
7AWS Academy IntegrationStrategy for institutional membership, cross-school deployment (Armour, Stuart, Computing, Design, Architecture), 17 certification pathway mappings to Elevate badges, Wednesday Smart Lab office hours, commitment-clause operating model, and Center of Excellence roadmap.
8Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform VisionProduct vision repositioning Elevate as a Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform Product; Talon backend roadmap; API integrations scope; technical requirements document; engineering co-design with Armour faculty and the India development team.
9Assessment Item Bank ArchitectureFramework-grounded item bank for activity forms (NACE, P21, WEF, CASEL, AAC&U HIPs, O*NET, DOE ESF, Kolb), with FERPA-safe localStorage drafting and Pearson Assessments catalog review (career/vocational/leadership/workplace-readiness only; clinical instruments explicitly out of scope).
10Crosswalk & Skills-by-Framework Matrices73-badge crosswalk against 8 frameworks with primary/secondary/supporting alignment coding; 12 proposed new badges with framework anchors and gap rationale; Top Skills by Framework page with 5 anchored scholarly definitions and citation patterns.
11Executive DashboardThis 40+-page analytics dashboard including all data visualizations (25+ Chart.js + SVG visualizations), narrative frameworks, monthly cockpit, month-toggle data layer, FERPA-safe reporting architecture, watermark + print rendering, and the executive-tile color treatments.

Usage Restrictions

Internal use only

This dashboard and its contents are confidential and intended exclusively for internal executive reporting at Illinois Institute of Technology.

Attribution required

Any reference to, presentation of, or adaptation of the frameworks, badge systems, partnership models, or operational processes documented herein must include explicit attribution to Dr. Freddrick Logan as original author and architect.

No unauthorized reproduction

Copying, redistributing, or presenting this work (in whole or in part) as one’s own is a violation of institutional intellectual property policies and professional ethics standards.

Derivative works

Any derivative works, adaptations, or extensions of the Elevate framework must acknowledge the original design and obtain approval from Dr. Freddrick Logan.

Institutional record

This intellectual property, including version history, development timeline, and authorship documentation, has been recorded with Illinois Institute of Technology. All versions are archived with timestamps demonstrating authorship provenance.

FERPA compliance

No raw student PII is stored in the dashboard source. Aggregated counts are default; first-name-only / role-gated views require staff authentication outside the static site. Form drafts on the Assessments page persist only in the student’s own browser (localStorage) and are never transmitted from this dashboard.

Framework Attribution, Trademarks & Citation

Framework attribution. The Elevate badge framework is grounded in publicly available, externally validated frameworks — NACE Career Readiness Competencies, P21 21st Century Skills, WEF Future of Jobs 2025, CASEL SEL, AAC&U High-Impact Practices, O*NET (US DOL), DOE Employability Skills Framework, Kolb Experiential Learning Theory, Coursera 2026 Job Skills Report, Harvard Pathways to Prosperity, HBS Managing the Future of Work, MIT Experiential Learning Office, MIT Teaching + Learning Lab, MIT Digital Credentials Consortium, Oxford Careers, Northeastern Co-op, Purdue-Gallup Index, Cincinnati Cooperative Education, Georgia Tech Experiential Education, Laanan’s Transfer Student Capital, Schlossberg’s Transition Theory, and Transfer Receptive Culture. Adaptations, crosswalks, and the 8-framework matrix are original Illinois Tech work product.

Trademarks. Illinois Tech and the Illinois Tech wordmark are trademarks of Illinois Institute of Technology. NACE, AAC&U, MIT, AWS, Microsoft, Accenture, Coursera, Pearson, Ed2Go, Credly, CampusGroups (312), Handshake, Talon, Braven, Sprintax, Pearson Assessments, Newzoo, Statista, and all other named partner organizations are trademarks of their respective owners. Use here is referential and academic.

Citation. When referencing the Elevate framework externally, please cite as: Logan, F. (2026). Elevate: Illinois Tech’s Workforce + Academic Hybrid Platform Product.
